source_doc:
Rink Rats
Chapter 1: First Day
Kyle looked at his reflection in the mirror. He studied the lines etched on his face, each one a reminder of the years gone by. A hint of gray threaded his hair now, and for a moment, he felt the weight of change settling in. 
He didn’t want to label it an identity crisis. Not yet at least. But as he splashed the cold sink water on his face, memories of his pro hockey career flooded his mind. The thrill of the game, the adrenaline of competition, the love of the fans. All forty years of his life had practically been dedicated to making it to the highest level. Now those days were behind him.
hypo_0:
溜冰场鼠
第一章：第一天
凯尔看着镜子中的倒影。他研究了脸上刻下的线条，每一条线都提醒着他过去的岁月。现在，他头发中夹杂着几缕灰色，他感觉到变化正在悄然发生。
他不想把这称为身份危机。至少现在还不想。但当他把冷水溅到脸上时，他脑海中涌现出他职业冰球生涯的回忆。比赛的刺激、竞争的肾上腺素、球迷的爱。他四十年的生命几乎都献给了最高水平的比赛。现在，那些日子已经成为过去。

source_doc:
It had been a remarkable twenty-year pro career, one that most players could only dream of. He wore a gleaming championship ring, a testament to his hard work and dedication. Kyle truly couldn’t have asked for more. With his wife and son by his side through every triumph and challenge, he was ready to step into this new chapter and return the favor they had shown him.
Today marked a significant milestone for the Barnes family; Ryan’s first day of school in their new town. After Kyle’s retirement, they had chosen to relocate to New Hampshire so Ryan could attend St. Cassian’s, the prestigious hockey prep school where Kyle had once played.

今天对巴恩斯一家来说是一个重要的日子，因为这是莱恩在他家的新城镇的第一天上学。在凯尔退役后，他们决定搬到新罕布什尔州，以便莱恩能够就读于圣卡西安中学，这是一所享有盛誉的冰球预备学校，凯尔也曾在这里就读。

source_doc:
It was usually his wife, Nicole, who ensured that milestones like this were executed flawlessly. But with his career behind him and plenty of free time ahead, they decided it was time to switch roles. After dedicating fifteen years to raising their son, Nicole was finally ready to return to work full-time and pursue her passion for interior design, leaving Kyle to navigate this unfamiliar routine on his own. 
Kyle knew he should be excited, yet uncertainty tugged at him. Taking a deep breath, he forced himself to focus. It was time for Nicole and Ryan to take the lead, with his unwavering support behind them. He was determined not to let them down.
hypo_0:
通常是他的妻子妮可确保这样的里程碑能够完美地完成。但是随着他的职业生涯结束，还有很多空闲时间在前面，他们决定是时候交换角色了。在致力于抚养儿子十五年后，妮可终于准备好全职工作，追求她对室内设计的热情，留下凯尔独自应对这个不熟悉的日常。
凯尔知道他应该感到兴奋，但还是有些不安。他深吸一口气，强迫自己集中注意力。现在是妮可和瑞安站出来领导的时候了，他会在背后坚定地支持他们。他决心不让她们失望。

“Fresh start,” he whispered to himself. “You got this.” 
Kyle followed the enticing aroma of fresh coffee to the kitchen. Nicole was in the final moments of her new morning routine, screwing the lid onto her to-go cup. When she caught sight of him out of the corner of her eye, she flashed him a quick, warm smile.
“Morning, Mr. Mom,” she said, her voice bright with excitement.
“Morning,” he chuckled, closing the distance between them and pressing a kiss to her lips. “Pretty sure morning coffee is my new responsibility.”
“You get a freebie today, rookie.” Nicole slung her purse over her shoulder and gave Kyle an encouraging pat on the backside as she passed him. “Alright, I’m outta here. Ryan needs to be downstairs in five minutes, and you two need to be out the door in ten. And don’t forget to snap at least one picture of him in his new uniform. I want a smile, or it doesn’t count!”

“重新开始，”他低声对自己说，“你能行。”
凯尔循着新鲜咖啡的诱人香气来到厨房。妮可正进行着她新的晨间例行公事，将盖子拧紧在她的外带杯上。当她余光瞥见他时，立刻对他露出一个快速而温暖的微笑。
“早上好，奶爸，”她声音里充满了兴奋。
“早上好，”他笑着，走近了他，在她嘴唇上轻轻一吻。“我觉得早上咖啡现在是我的新职责了。”

“I’ll make sure he gets to school on time. No promises about the smiling part, though.”
“If he behaves for the picture, tell him we can hit the diner after practice.”
“Deal.” Kyle nodded. “Go get ’em, babe.”
Nicole winked at him as she opened the door to the garage. “Love you. Keep me updated!”
And just like that, she was gone. The sudden silence was almost unsettling, prompting Kyle to pour himself a cup of coffee to distract his restless thoughts.
“Hey, Ry! You about ready?” Kyle called up the stairs.
Silence followed, typical for a teenager. Kyle settled at the table for the time being, scrolling through his phone. A new text pinged, and he tapped on it.

source_doc:
“Barnesy.” It was from his old teammate, Luc Benoit, now coaching St. Cassian’s hockey team. “Need a favor. Meet me at the rink soon?” 
“Already need my advice on day one, Benny?” Kyle shot a teasing text back.
“Just trying to keep you busy, old man.” 
Kyle smiled at the jab. Even after all these years, their relationship had remained as lively and playful as it was in their twenties.
”Be there in fifteen. Bout to drop off Ry.” 
“Shit,” he muttered under his breath when he checked the time. “Ryan William!” he yelled up the stairs. “If we’re late on your first day, your mom is going to skin me, and then I’m gonna skin you! Let’s go!”

“巴恩斯。”短信来自他的老队友卢克·伯努瓦，如今在圣卡西安冰球队当教练。“帮个忙。能尽快来冰场碰头吗？”
“第一天就急着求指教啦，本尼？”凯尔揶揄地回了条消息。
“就想给老家伙找点事做。”  
凯尔对着这句调侃笑起来。即便过了这么多年，他们的关系还像二十多岁时那样鲜活生动。
“十五分钟到。正要送瑞恩上学。”  
他瞥了眼时间，低声咒骂。“瑞恩·威廉！”他冲着楼梯上方吼道，“要是你开学第一天就迟到，你妈会剥了我的皮，然后我再剥了你的皮！赶紧的！”

“巴恩斯。”发信人是他昔日队友卢克·贝努瓦，现在是圣卡西安冰球队的教练。“有个忙，能来冰场见我吗？”
“第一天就需要我出主意了，贝尼？”凯尔调侃地回了条短信。
“只是想让你别太闲，老家伙。”
凯尔对这句玩笑笑了笑。多年过去，他们的关系依然像二十多岁时那样活泼有趣。
“十五分钟到，先送瑞安。”
他看了眼时间，低声咕哝了句：“糟了。”然后大声喊道：“瑞安·威廉！要是你第一天就迟到，你妈会扒了我的皮，然后我再扒你的皮！快点！”

凯尔对这条短信笑了笑。即使过了这么多年，他们的关系依然像二十岁时那样充满活力和嬉戏。
“十五点到。要把瑞安送下去。”
“糟糕，”他低声嘟囔着，看了看时间。“瑞安·威廉！”他朝楼梯上喊道。“如果我们迟到了，你妈妈会把我打死，然后我会把你打死！我们走！”

“第一天就需要我的建议了，本尼？”凯尔回了条调侃的消息。
“只是想让你这个老家伙别闲着。”
凯尔对这句玩笑话笑了。这么多年过去了，他们的关系依然像二十多岁时那样活泼有趣。
“十五分钟后到。正准备送瑞安过去。”
他低头看了看时间，小声嘀咕道：“该死。”然后朝楼上喊道：“瑞安·威廉！要是第一天就迟到，你妈会剥了我的皮，然后我再剥了你的！快点！”

“I’m coming!” 
Kyle pushed back from the table and swiftly poured his coffee from his favorite mug into a to-go cup. A moment later, the sound of hurried footsteps echoed down the stairs behind him. He grabbed his keys from the counter and turned to face his son.
“Got everything?” Kyle asked. He took in the sight in front of him; his heart swelling with pride and a touch of nostalgia seeing his old school colors on his boy. 
“Yeah,” Ryan replied, tucking his school polo into his khaki pants. He noticed the warm expression on his dad’s face. “What’s with the look?”

“Royal blue and gold really suit you, Champ.”
Ryan smirked and rolled his eyes. “You’re getting so soft in your old age.” 
“Is that so?” 
With a playful grin, Kyle reached out to pinch the crook of Ryan’s neck, a gesture that had always made his son laugh.
Ryan burst into genuine laughter, tucking his chin and batting at Kyle’s hand to dodge the attack. “Okay, okay!”
“That’s what I thought. Come on, let’s get moving.”
Kyle smiled and gave Ryan’s backside a light swat, nudging him along. They stepped outside, where the morning sun cast a warm glow over their freshly painted house, a vivid reminder of their recent move back to New England.

“皇家蓝和金色真的很适合你，冠军。”
瑞恩嘴角一扬，翻了个白眼，“你老了怎么越来越肉麻了。”
“是吗？”
凯尔露出一抹调皮的笑容，伸手去捏瑞恩的脖子弯，这个动作总能让儿子笑起来。
瑞恩真的笑了出来，低头躲闪，拍打着凯尔的手，“好啦好啦！”
“果然如此。走吧，我们出发了。”
凯尔笑着轻轻拍了拍瑞恩的屁股，推着他往前走。他们走出门外，晨光温暖地洒在刚刷过的房子上，生动地提醒着他们最近搬回新英格兰的日子。

source_doc:
The atmosphere in the car was charged with a blend of anticipation and anxiety. Kyle could sense Ryan’s nervous energy radiating beside him, and he couldn’t help but remember his own first day, driven by his dad to this very school. He understood, probably more than anyone, the mix of excitement and fear his son was feeling.
As they pulled into the school parking lot, Kyle exhaled, taking in the stunning, castle-like buildings. He remembered just how much he loved this campus, especially in the fall when the foliage was at its peak.
“Ready, buddy?” Kyle asked, glancing over as Ryan fiddled with his backpack in the passenger seat.

车内的气氛充满了期待和紧张的混合情绪。凯尔能感觉到瑞安在他旁边散发出的紧张能量，不禁回想起自己第一天的情景——当时是父亲开车送他来的这所学校。他很理解瑞安此刻的复杂心情——既兴奋又害怕，也许比任何人都更能感同身受。
当他们驶入学校停车场时，凯尔呼出一口气，饱览着令人惊艳的、像城堡一样雄伟的建筑。他记得自己有多么喜欢这个校园，尤其是在秋天，树叶茂盛，景色达到巅峰。
“准备好了吗，孩子？”凯尔问道，同时瞥了一眼瑞安，后者正摆弄着乘客座位上的背包。

“Yeah, I think so,” Ryan replied, his voice steady despite the nervous flutter in his stomach. There was a determination in his eyes that reassured Kyle.
“Hey, you’ll do great,” Kyle said, offering a comforting smile. “But before you head to class, Mom wants a quick picture.”
Ryan groaned, anticipating the familiar routine. “Do we have to—”
“Ryan,” Kyle replied, his tone light but firm. “Less attitude, please. You know the drill.”
They stepped out of the car, and Kyle quickly snapped a picture he knew Nicole would cherish. It wasn’t Ryan’s most enthusiastic smile, but it was a smile nonetheless.

source_doc:
“I’ll see you at practice, okay?” Kyle wrapped an arm around Ryan’s shoulders, pulling him close and pressing a kiss to his soft, blonde curls. “Have a great day. Love you.”
“Love you too.”
Kyle stood for a moment, watching as Ryan disappeared through the arched doorway of the main building. As the door swung shut behind his son, Kyle took a deep breath, knowing this was just the beginning of a new journey for them both.
After a lifetime of strict schedules, the idea of an open day felt both liberating and daunting. So when Luc’s text came through this morning, Kyle was genuinely relieved, and a little thrilled, to have somewhere to be.

He made his way across campus toward the Griffin Den, the school’s storied ice rink, coffee in hand. The crisp morning air and familiar surroundings stirred something deep in his chest.
Kyle pushed open the doors to the rink, and the familiar scent of ice, rubber, and old gear hit him instantly. For the first time that day, he felt like himself. He stood in the stillness, letting the silence wash over him as he gazed out at the freshly zambonied sheet of ice, smooth and untouched, like a blank page waiting to be written on. 
Kyle glanced up at the banners hanging from the rafters, his eyes quickly finding the year he and Luc had won their championship. The sight pulled him back in time, memories flooding in all at once, sharp and vivid, like the ice was still beneath his skates.
hypo_0:
他手捧咖啡，穿过校园，朝“格里芬冰场”走去——那是学校里历史悠久、充满传奇色彩的溜冰场。清晨清新的空气与熟悉的环境激起了他内心深处某种情感。
凯尔推开冰场的大门，冰面、橡胶与旧装备混合在一起的熟悉气味瞬间扑面而来。这是他当天第一次感到自己仿佛真正回到了原本的模样。他静静地站在那里，任凭周围的寂静包围自己，凝望着那片刚刚被清理过的冰面——光滑无瑕，宛如一张等待书写的白纸。
他抬头望向悬挂在天花板上的锦旗，目光很快锁定在那面记录着他与卢克夺冠年份的横幅上。这一幕将他瞬间拉回过去，记忆如潮水般涌来，清晰而生动，仿佛冰面依旧在他冰鞋之下。
hypo_1:
他端着咖啡，穿过校园，走向格里芬之穴——学校那充满传奇色彩的冰场。清爽的晨间空气和熟悉的环境搅动着他内心深处的情感。
凯尔推开冰场的大门，那股熟悉的冰、橡胶和旧装备的气味立刻扑面而来。那天，他第一次感觉自己又做回了自己。他静静地站着，任凭寂静笼罩着他，凝视着那片刚被冰面修整车清理过的冰面，光滑无痕，像一张等待书写的白纸。
凯尔抬头看着悬挂在椽子上的旗帜，他的目光很快找到了他和卢克赢得冠军的那一年。那景象把他拉回了过去，记忆一下子涌上心头，清晰而生动，仿佛冰面仍在他的冰鞋之下。

凯尔推开冰球场的门，熟悉的冰、橡胶和旧装备的气味瞬间扑面而来。那天他第一次感到像自己一样。他站在寂静中，让沉默笼罩着他，凝视着新鲜打磨过的冰面，光滑而未触碰，就像一张等待书写的空白页。
凯尔抬头看着横梁上悬挂的横幅，他的目光迅速找到了他和卢克赢得冠军的那一年。这景象把他带回了过去，记忆一下子涌了上来，清晰而生动，就好像冰还在他的冰鞋下一样。

凯尔推开溜冰场的大门，一股混杂着冰面、橡胶和旧装备的熟悉气味立刻扑面而来。这是今天他第一次，感觉自己又做回了自己。他静静地站着，任凭寂静将他包裹，目光凝视着那片刚刚被冰面整理车清理过的冰面，平滑如新，未留一丝痕迹，宛如一张等待书写的白纸。
凯尔抬头瞥了一眼悬挂在椽梁上的冠军旗帜，他的目光迅速找到了他和卢克赢得冠军那一年的旗帜。这一幕将他拉回了过去，记忆一下子如潮水般涌来，清晰而生动，就仿佛冰面仍然在他的冰鞋之下。

Suddenly, a voice cut through his thoughts. “The good ol’ days, eh, Barnesy?”
Kyle turned to see Luc emerging from his office, a grin on his face as he walked over.
Kyle resisted the urge to roll his eyes at that grin. It was annoying as hell that Luc still looked like he belonged on the cover of GQ , even after a grueling pro hockey career of his own.
“Just what I needed, another reminder that I’m getting old,” Kyle said with a chuckle. “It’s surreal being back. Seeing all this again…it feels like a lifetime ago.”
“You’ll get used to it,” Luc said with a smile, reaching out for a firm handshake and pulling Kyle into a quick shoulder-clap hug, the kind of greeting that hadn’t changed since they were teammates.

突然，一个声音打断了他的思绪。“怀念过去的美好时光了吧，巴恩西？”
凯尔转过身，看到卢克从办公室里走出来，脸上带着笑容朝他走来。
凯尔强忍住翻白眼的冲动。令他无奈的是，即使经历了漫长而艰苦的职业冰球生涯，卢克看上去依然像是《GQ》杂志的封面人物。
“我正需要这个——又一个提醒我正在变老的信号，”凯尔轻笑着说道，“回到这里感觉很不真实。再看到这一切……仿佛已是上辈子的事。”
“你会习惯的，”卢克微笑着说，伸出手与他紧紧握手，随后拍了拍他的肩膀，给了他一个简短而熟悉的拥抱——这种问候方式，从他们还是队友时起就从未改变过。

Kyle noticed the file in Luc's hand, its edges worn from use. “What’s this? Got a scouting report for me?” he asked, curiosity piqued as he leaned in slightly, eager to hear what Luc had to share.
“This,” Luc hummed as he tapped the file with his free hand, “is the favor. I’ll fill you in on the details. Come on, let’s sit.”
Kyle looked momentarily puzzled but settled onto the cold metal bleachers beside him.
”So, we’ve got a last-minute addition, and usually we don’t make exceptions, right? Once the roster is finalized over the summer, that’s typically it,” Luc began. “But this kid is something special. I’m telling you, man, he’s got insane potential.”

"这个，"卢克一边用空着的手轻拍文件夹一边嗯了一声，"就是那个人情。我来跟你说说详细情况。来，我们坐下说。"
凯尔看起来有些困惑，但还是在他旁边冰冷的金属看台上坐了下来。
"是这样的，我们有个临时加入的，通常我们是不破例的，对吧？夏天名单一旦确定，基本上就是这样了，"卢克开始说道。"但这孩子很特别。我跟你说，兄弟，他的潜力简直惊人。"

Luc handed the file to Kyle, who opened it with interest. "Declan Avery Hull" was printed in bold letters across the top, accompanied by a photo paperclipped to the top right corner. Kyle mentally noted the boyish features: deep green eyes, shiny, long, floppy brunette hair, and a bold expression that suggested confidence. His gaze then shifted to the hockey resume, and he raised his eyebrows at its impressive details.
Kyle let out a low whistle, the sound escaping him like a mix of surprise and admiration. “Damn, this kid is going to be a stud.”
“Just wait until you see him play, he arrives tomorrow,” Luc said, his tone shifting slightly. “But I do have some concerns.”

凯尔低声吹了声口哨，声音中夹杂着惊讶和钦佩。“天哪，这孩子以后肯定是个明星。”  
“等你看到他打球就知道了，他明天就到。”卢克语气略变。“但我确实有些担心。”

“With what?” Kyle asked, unable to find anything amiss in the details his eyes were scanning on the page.
Luc flipped to the back, where the more personal information was stored, and pointed to the notes section with his finger.
Kyle read the first bullet point: “Off-ice behavior must be addressed if he wants to reach his full potential.”
His eyebrows instinctively raised at that, a knowing look passing between them as he turned to Luc. “Sounds like he’s a bit of a wildcard, huh?”
Luc sighed, shaking his head as he fell deep into thought. “I don’t want to make excuses for him. Hell, I don’t even know him yet. But the kid has been bouncing from boarding school to boarding school for most of his childhood. That can’t be helping, right? I think what he really needs is stability.”

“Where are the parents?” Kyle asked, the question feeling all too obvious given his own experiences. 
He and Nicole had made it a priority to effectively manage their son’s off-ice behavior throughout his upbringing, committed to ensuring no negative bullet points marred his hockey resume. Any acting out was always nipped in the bud, immediately.
“Mom’s not in the picture; hasn’t been since he was a baby, from what I hear,” Luc replied. “And the dad isn’t very present; he doesn’t show up to any games. Just writes the checks when he needs to.”
“Shit,” was all Kyle could manage in response.

他和妮可一直把有效管理儿子在冰场外的行为放在首位，致力于确保他的冰球生涯中没有任何负面记录。在他成长的过程中，任何不当行为都会被立即扼杀在萌芽状态。

Kyle cut in, finishing Luc’s thought. “You want us to be his billet family.”
Luc nodded thoughtfully. “I’ve seen firsthand how you and Nicole raised Ryan, and just look at how incredible he turned out. If Declan has a support system like yours, it could be a game changer for him.”
Kyle took a moment to ponder. This wasn’t merely a simple favor; this was a responsibility for another soul. He hadn’t anticipated considering taking in a kid when he read Luc’s text that morning. Still, he would do anything for Luc, and the earnest look in his eyes made it clear just how serious this was to him. That meant it was now serious to Kyle as well.

“I hear you, and I appreciate that you think so highly of me,” Kyle said, a teasing smile playing on his lips. “Let me chat with Nicole and Ryan over dinner tonight, and I’ll get back to you, okay? I’m confident we can work something out.”
“I really appreciate it, man,” Luc said earnestly. “As a thank you for even considering it, feel free to use the gym here whenever you want. We don’t want you developing a dad bod, now do we?” he teased, giving Kyle’s stomach a playful pat. 
“Don’t you dare start that with me, you little sh—” Kyle retorted, unable to suppress a grin.

“Got a meeting to run to!” Luc exclaimed, rising from the bleachers and jogging toward the coaches office before Kyle could finish his thought. “Keep that file tonight, just bring it back tomorrow. Love you!”
“Yeah, yeah,” Kyle said, rolling his eyes with a chuckle.
A mix of emotions stirred within Kyle as he found himself alone in the rink once more. The weight of the decision ahead loomed large, but he felt a flicker of hope. If there was a chance to make a difference for Declan, perhaps it was worth taking the leap.
He took a deep breath, glancing around the rink where memories flooded back once again: laughter, camaraderie, and the family he cherished. This new chapter was unfolding, a fresh purpose, and maybe it was finally time to embrace the unknown.

当凯尔再次独自一人站在冰场上时，他内心涌起一股复杂的情绪。即将做出的决定让他感到压力巨大，但他也隐约感到一丝希望。如果他有机会帮助迪克兰，或许值得冒险一试。
他深吸一口气，环顾四周，冰场上的一切都让他回忆起过去：欢笑、友谊，以及他所珍视的家人。新的篇章即将展开，他有了新的目标，或许现在是时候勇敢地迎接未知了。

当凯尔再次独自一人待在冰场时，内心涌起了复杂的情绪。即将面临的抉择仿佛一块沉重的石头压在心头，但他心中仍燃着一丝希望。如果有机会为德克兰带来转机，也许值得冒险一试。  
他深吸一口气，环顾着这片冰场，往事如潮水般涌来：欢声笑语、深厚的友情，还有他珍视如家人的队友们……人生的新篇章正在展开，新的目标就在眼前——或许，是时候拥抱未知了。

“Okay, we’ve got two classic burgers, one club sandwich, and fries all around,” the waitress announced cheerfully as she placed each plate on the table with practiced ease. “Can I get you anything else?”
“I think we’re all set, thanks, Jo,” Kyle said with a warm smile as he shifted his napkin onto his lap.
The Barnes family began digging in after the waitress left them to it. Ryan immediately reached for the ketchup and, true to form, drowned his fries in an absurd amount, completely unfazed by the amused glances from his parents.
Without a word, Kyle plucked the pickles from his burger and placed them on Ryan’s plate; a quiet ritual that had long become second nature. It was a small thing, but moments like that made Kyle feel grounded, like some parts of life hadn’t changed at all.

“我们都齐了，谢谢你，乔。”凯尔温和地笑着，把餐巾铺在腿上。
女服务员离开后，巴恩斯一家开始享用美食。瑞安第一时间拿起番茄酱，像往常一样把薯条浇得满满的，完全不在意父母投来的好笑目光。

The table fell into a comfortable silence as everyone tucked into their meals, the clinking of silverware and the occasional satisfied hum filling the space between them. After a few minutes of quiet eating, Kyle leaned back slightly and broke the silence, ready to reconnect with his family.
“Alright,” he hummed, taking a sip of his drink before setting the glass down. “Big day for all of us, huh? Who’s up first, anyone want to share?”
”Oh, I’ll go,” Nicole said, her eyes lighting up. “As much as I love you, my beautiful boys, it was so nice to be back at the office today. Everyone was incredibly welcoming. Today was mostly administrative stuff, but we also talked through some upcoming projects, and I’m already itching to dive in.”

Kyle smiled, genuinely happy to see her so energized. “That’s awesome, babe. You’ve been waiting a long time for this.”
“Yeah,” Ryan added between bites. “Mom’s finally getting to make other people’s houses look as cool as ours.”
“Exactly!” Nicole cooed, giving Ryan’s bicep a soft pinch. “Though I do still expect your help moving samples around the garage, Mr. Muscles.”
Ryan groaned with a grin. “Knew that was coming.”
Kyle chuckled at their banter, then shifted in his seat, draping his arm around the back of Ryan’s chair so he could fully face him. As he moved, he gave the back of Ryan’s head a gentle scruff, earning a mock-annoyed eye roll from his son.

“是啊，”瑞安边吃边补充道。“妈妈终于可以把别人的房子装扮得和我们家一样漂亮了。”
“没错！”妮可娇声说道，轻轻捏了捏瑞安的肱二头肌。“不过，我还是期待你帮忙在车库里搬运样品，肌肉先生。”
瑞安咧嘴一笑，露出不满的表情。“就知道你会这么说。”
凯尔对他们的玩笑笑了起来，然后在座位上挪动了一下，把胳膊搭在瑞安椅子的后面，这样他就可以完全面对他。当他移动时，他轻轻地捏了捏瑞安的后脑勺，这让他儿子不屑地翻了个白眼。

“Alright, your turn, Champ. First day at St. Cassian’s, how’d it go?”
Ryan shrugged, poking at a fry. “It was alright.”
“Just alright?” Nicole asked, raising an eyebrow. 
“I mean, it’s school. But the campus is pretty sick, and a couple of the guys on the team seemed cool. Classes weren’t bad either.”
Kyle nodded, his gaze steady. “That’s a solid start.”
Ryan gave another shrug, but this time it came with a small smile. “Yeah, I guess it wasn’t the worst first day.”
Kyle began rubbing a slow circle between Ryan’s shoulder blades absentmindedly, his gaze drifting to the window beside them. The sun was beginning to dip below the trees, casting golden streaks across the diner’s parking lot, a reminder of how quickly time was already passing.

“我是说，毕竟是学校嘛。不过校园挺酷的，队里有几个家伙看起来也挺不错的，课程也还能接受。”

“Hey,” Kyle hummed, giving Ryan’s shoulder a light squeeze to catch his attention. “Tell your mom about practice. She’s been waiting all day to hear about it.”
Ryan perked up, his eyes brightening a little. “The rink’s insane, it’s like NHL-level. Uncle Luc- I mean, Coach Luc had us doing some light drills, mostly puck work and conditioning,” he said, trying to keep his voice even, though the excitement peeked through. 
“Yeah?” Nicole leaned in, genuinely curious. “Did it feel like the right fit?”
Ryan nodded. “Yeah. Everyone’s really good, but I think I held my own. Coach Luc kept it pretty easy on us for the first day, but I know it’s gonna ramp up fast.”

“Technically,” she teased, leaning back with a grin. “I have half a mind to make you retake it tomorrow.”
“Nope,” Ryan said quickly, already shaking his head. “One per semester. That’s the rule.”
Kyle chuckled into his drink. “He’s been keeping track, apparently.”

“Yeah, yeah,” Kyle said quickly, waving a hand to ease the concern. “It’s nothing bad. Just…something Luc brought up this morning when I saw him at the rink.”
Ryan glanced up from his fries, picking up on the subtle shift in his dad’s tone. His posture straightened slightly, curiosity taking hold.
“He asked me for a favor,” Kyle continued, glancing between them. “And it’s kind of a big one.”
Ryan raised an eyebrow, still chewing a fry. “Like, helping him move a couch kind of favor, or helping bury a body kind of favor?”
Nicole shot Ryan a disapproving look, but Kyle couldn’t help but chuckle. “It’s somewhere in between, I think.”

Ryan leaned back in his chair, taking a sip of his drink. “Okay, well now I’m really curious. What is it?” 
Kyle shifted in his seat, removing his arm from the back of Ryan's chair and leaning forward slightly, resting his forearms on the table. “Luc asked if we’d consider being a billet family this season, for a new player joining the team.”
Nicole paused, setting down her glass with a thoughtful clink. “He wants us to take in a kid?” Her expression morphed, less surprise and more intrigue. “That’s definitely not something Luc would ask lightly.”
Kyle nodded. “Exactly. This kid, Declan Hull, he’s talented, no question. But his home life’s rocky. Luc thinks living with a stable family could really ground him.”

“Luc said things came together really last minute, the paperwork just cleared. Declan gets in tomorrow, but he’s already locked in on the roster.”
Ryan tilted his head, clearly still processing. “Huh…that’s kind of wild. Just showing up outta nowhere.”
Kyle gently bumped his shoulder into Ryan’s, his voice steady. “Life isn’t always neat, Ry. This kid’s been bounced around for years, not because he’s a bad kid, but because no one’s given him a real chance. This might be his shot at something solid. So let’s not judge him before we even meet him, okay?”

Ryan pouted slightly at the soft scolding, his shoulders sinking just a bit. He looked down at his plate, quiet for a moment as he pushed a fry through the last streak of ketchup. 
“So he’s my age?” he asked, his voice more thoughtful now.
Kyle nodded. “Same grade. He’ll turn fifteen in May,” he said, watching Ryan closely for a reaction.
But nothing came. Ryan simply continued to eat the last of his fries, prompting Kyle to press on. “You okay with that?” he asked gently. “It’s a big shift, I know.”
Ryan shrugged, then nodded slowly. “I mean…yeah. I get it. If he really needs it, I’m not gonna be a jerk about it. Just gotta wrap my head around having a roommate now, I guess.”

“他和我一样大吗？”他现在语气更认真了。  
凯尔点头。“同一个年级。他五月就满十五岁了。”他密切观察着瑞安的反应。

瑞安耸耸肩，然后慢慢点头。“嗯……好吧。我懂。如果他真的需要，我不会当个混蛋。只是得适应一下现在有个室友这件事吧。”

Before Kyle could respond, Nicole quickly interjected. “If you weren’t okay with it, that wouldn’t make you a jerk, sweetheart. Do you understand? It’s natural to feel unsure, and you have the biggest heart I’ve ever seen, even when you try to hide it.”
Ryan gave a half-smile, eyes flicking toward his plate again.
Nicole glanced at Kyle, then back to Ryan. “And if this boy really needs a place to land, I think we’d be the right kind of home for him. It won’t be perfect, and it’ll probably take some adjusting, but we’ve got more to give. We always have.”

source_doc:
She looked at Kyle now, and his heart swelled. He reached for Nicole’s hand under the table and gave it a gentle squeeze.
”I think it’s worth a shot, yeah?” Kyle nodded, his voice steady as he looked between the two of them. “But just so there’s no confusion, family comes first. It always has, and it always will.”
Nicole gave a small, knowing smile, and Ryan met his dad’s eyes, listening closely.
“We’ll do our best to make this work,” Kyle continued, “but if it doesn’t feel right, if it’s too much or causes strain in the house, then we reassess. No guilt. No pressure. Just honesty.”

“我觉得这值得一试，对吧？”凯尔点点头，他的声音坚定，目光在两人之间来回扫视。“但为了避免误解，家庭永远是第一位的。它一直是这样，也永远会是这样。”
尼科尔露出了一个小小的、意味深长的微笑，而瑞恩则专注地看着他的父亲，仔细聆听着。
“我们会尽力让这事成功，”凯尔继续说道，“但如果感觉不对，如果压力太大或者给家庭带来负担，我们就重新评估。没有内疚。没有压力。只有诚实。”

He paused for a beat. “But something tells me we might be exactly what this kid needs.”
Nicole reached for her water and nodded. “And maybe he’s exactly what we need too.”
Ryan leaned back in his seat, nudging his empty plate aside. “So…does finding out I’ve got a roommate now at least get me a to-go milkshake or something?”
Nicole raised an eyebrow, clearly amused. “Oh, we’re negotiating now?”
“I mean, emotional growth is exhausting,” Ryan said with a grin. “I think I’ve earned some sugar.”
Kyle laughed, reaching for the check. “Fair enough. One chocolate milkshake, extra whipped cream. You definitely earned it.”

妮可拿起水杯点了点头。“也许他正是我们所需要的。”
瑞恩靠在座位上,把空盘子推到一边。“那么……现在我有了室友,至少能得到一个外卖奶昔吗？”
妮可抬起眉毛，显然很好笑。“哦，我们现在在谈判吗？”
“我是说，情感成长很累人，”瑞恩笑着说。“我想我应该得到一些糖分。”
凯尔笑了，拿起了账单。“公平点。一杯巧克力奶昔，加奶油。你确实应该得到。”

source_doc:
As they stood and gathered their things, the air around them felt lighter. They stepped out of the diner as one, Kyle’s arm draped protectively over Ryan’s shoulders, his other hand gently clasping Nicole’s.
There was still a lot unknown about what lay ahead, but they were facing it together.
And that, Kyle thought, was more than a good start.
The house was quiet again. The kind of quiet that settles in only after a full day. Kyle padded barefoot down the hallway, coffee mug in one hand, Declan’s file tucked under his arm.
The bedroom light cast a warm glow across the sheets as Nicole emerged from the bathroom, brushing out the last bit of moisture from her hair. She wore one of Kyle’s old team t-shirts, the fabric soft and oversized, sleeves brushing her elbows.
hypo_0:
他们起身收拾东西时，周围的空气似乎变得轻盈起来。三人步出小餐馆时宛如一体——凯尔的手臂保护性地搭在瑞恩肩上，另一只手则温柔地握着妮可的手。
前路依然充满未知，但他们将共同面对。
而凯尔心想，这已经远不止是个良好的开端了。
房子再次安静下来。那种只有经过一整天的喧嚣后才会降临的宁静。凯尔光着脚走过走廊，一手端着咖啡杯，腋下夹着迪克兰的档案袋。
当妮可从浴室出来时，卧室的灯光在床单上投下温暖的光晕。她正用梳子理顺发梢最后几缕湿气，身上套着凯尔的旧球队T恤。棉质布料柔软宽大，袖口垂落在她的手肘处。

source_doc:
Kyle was already propped up against the headboard when she climbed into bed beside him, pulling the covers over her lap with a contented sigh.
“Ry asleep?” Nicole murmured, easing into his side, her fingers brushing lightly against his arm.
“Mhm,” Kyle hummed, setting his mug down on the nightstand with a soft clink. The folder remained open on his lap, Declan’s profile page catching the soft pool of lamplight. Nicole’s gaze drifted to it.
“You’ve read that three times already,” she said softly.
“Yeah,” Kyle admitted, eyes still on the page. “I keep thinking about how young he looks. He’s just a kid. But there’s something in his eyes…I don’t know. It’s like he’s already carrying the weight of someone twice his age.”

当妮可爬上床，把被子拉到大腿上，发出满足的叹息时，凯尔已经靠在床头了。
“瑞睡着了？”妮可低声问，靠向他的身旁，手指轻轻地刷过他的手臂。
“嗯，”凯尔哼了一声，把杯子放到床头柜上，发出轻微的碰击声。档案夹仍然打开着，放在他的大腿上，德克兰的个人资料页在柔和的灯光下闪耀。妮可的目光飘向了它。
“你已经读了三遍了，”她轻声说。
“是的，”凯尔承认，眼睛仍然盯着那页纸。“我一直在想他看上去有多年轻。他只是一个孩子。但他的眼睛里……我不知道。就好像他已经承载了比他年龄大一倍的重量。”

“嗯。”凯尔低应着，将马克杯轻轻搁在床头柜上，发出细微的碰撞声。文件夹仍摊开在他膝头，德克兰的资料页在台灯柔光下格外醒目。妮可的目光落在那页纸上。

source_doc:
Nicole leaned her head against his shoulder, her hand finding his on the file and resting there. “You’re already in it, huh?”
Kyle let out a breath through his nose; half a laugh, half a sigh. “I don’t know. Maybe. I just…I keep picturing what it would’ve been like for me at his age without my dad showing up to every game. Without someone giving a damn.”
Nicole was quiet for a moment, her thumb gently tracing his knuckles.
“We’re good at giving a damn,” she said finally, her voice barely above a whisper.
Kyle turned to look at her, his expression soft. “You sure you’re up for this? Things were just starting to feel…balanced.”

凯尔从鼻子里叹了口气，一半是笑，一半是叹。“我不知道。也许吧。我只是…我一直在想象，如果我那时和他一样大的年纪，没有我爸去参加每一次比赛会是什么样子。如果没有人真正关心。”
妮可沉默了一会儿，拇指轻轻地摩挲着他的指节。
“我们会关心，”她终于轻声说，声音几乎低不可闻。
凯尔转过头看着她，表情柔和。“你确定你能应付吗？事情刚刚开始变得…平衡起来。”
hypo_1:
妮可把头靠在他肩上，手搭在他手上，停在资料夹上。“你已经投入进去了，对吗？”
凯尔鼻子里呼出一口气，半笑半叹。“也许吧。我总忍不住想，如果我小时候没有我爸每场都来，没有人在乎我，会是什么样。”
妮可沉默片刻，拇指轻轻摩挲着他的指关节。
“我们很擅长在乎别人。”她终于低声说。
凯尔转头看着她，神情温柔。“你确定你准备好了吗？生活刚刚才恢复平衡。”

凯尔从鼻子里呼出一口气；一半是笑声，一半是叹息。“我不知道。也许吧。我就是……一直想象如果我像他那个年纪时，没有爸爸出现在每场比赛中，没有人关心我的话，会是什么样的情景。”
妮可沉默了一会儿，她的拇指轻轻地抚摸着他的指关节。
“我们很擅长关心别人，”她终于开口了，声音几乎是耳语。
凯尔转过头来看着她，表情很柔和。“你确定你能应付得了这件事吗？我们的生活才刚刚开始……步入正轨。”

“I’m sure,” she said, meeting his eyes. “We’ve always made room when it mattered. This matters.”
“And Ryan?” Kyle’s voice dipped, laced with quiet guilt. “I promised him this next chapter would be about him. More time together, just the two of us; his hockey, his moment. And now, day one, I’m asking him to share all of that with a complete stranger.”
“What if it’s good for him too?” Nicole asked gently, her voice thoughtful. “Learning how to make space for someone else. Figuring out who he is not just on the ice, but as a teammate, a friend. Maybe even a brother.”
hypo_0:
“我确定，”她说，眼神中充满了坚定。“我们总是在关键时刻给他留出位置。这一次也不例外。”
“那瑞恩呢？”凯尔的声音低沉，带着沉重的内疚。“我承诺下一章会写到他。我们两个在一起的时间更多，只有我们两个；他的曲棍球，他的时刻。现在，第一天，我却要他把这一切都与一个陌生人分享。”
“如果这对他也有好处呢？”妮可轻声问道，她的声音充满思考。“学会如何为别人留出位置。不仅在冰场上，还要作为队友、朋友，甚至是兄弟，弄清楚他到底是谁。”

source_doc:
Kyle hadn’t considered that angle, not really. The idea settled over him slowly, unexpected but not unwelcome, like a shift in light that made the whole picture look different.
“Damn,” Kyle murmured, a quiet smile tugging at his lips. “You’re good.”
Nicole grinned, tilting her head as she bumped her shoulder against his. “That’s why you married me, Barnes.”
“Among a million other reasons,” he said, leaning over to press a kiss to her temple. “I love you.”
She smirked sleepily. “I love you too. Now turn off that light. We’ve got another big day tomorrow.” 
Kyle chuckled, snapping the folder shut. He placed it gently on the nightstand beside his mug, then clicked off the lamp.

凯尔确实没从这个角度想过。这个想法慢慢在他心里沉淀下来，虽然出乎意料，却并不让人排斥，就像光线变幻，让整幅画面都呈现出不同的样貌。
“真有你的，”凯尔轻声说，一丝微笑牵动了嘴角。“你真厉害。”
妮可咧嘴一笑，歪着头用肩膀撞了撞他。“要不你干嘛娶我呢，巴恩斯。”
“当然还有其他无数个理由，”他说道，俯身在她太阳穴上印下一个吻。“我爱你。”
她睡意朦胧地俏皮一笑。“我也爱你。好了，快关灯吧。我们明天还有得忙呢。”
凯尔轻笑一声，把文件夹“啪”地合上了。他把它轻轻放在床头柜的杯子旁，然后“咔哒”一声关掉了台灯。

As darkness settled around them, he lay back beside her, one arm beneath her shoulders, the steady rhythm of her breathing already beginning to slow.
And for a long while, Kyle stared at the ceiling, wide awake.
Tomorrow, everything would begin again. New roles. New routines. A new kid.
A new chance.

黑暗降临在他们周围，他躺在她身边，一只胳膊放在她肩下，她稳定的呼吸节奏已经开始变慢。
凯尔长时间盯着天花板，精神高度集中。
明天，一切又要从头开始了。新的角色。新的日常。一个新孩子。
一个新的机会。

而凯尔则长时间地盯着天花板，清醒着。  
明天，一切将重新开始。新的角色，新的日常，一个新的孩子。  
一个新的机会。

明天，一切又要从头开始。新的角色。新的日常。新的孩子。
一份新的机会。

明天，一切都将重新开始。新的角色。新的日常。一个新的孩子。
一个新的机会。

source_doc: Sue Kay of Hawke's Bay, New Zealand writes: "I wish to thank all the kind people who helped my husband and me find our way from the airport to our hotel in the Sydney CBD. My husband is 90, and I'm 74 but, despite making ourselves known to our guide at the airport on arrival, we were left behind! We didn't know how to get to our hotel as we had the transfer organised. We must have asked at least six different people how to get to the Furama and everybody was so helpful and even helped us with our luggage. Sydneysiders may be big-city slickers but they have a small-town kindness!"
hypo_0: 新西兰霍克斯湾的苏·凯写道：“我要感谢所有帮助我和我丈夫从机场找到去市中心酒店路线的好心人。我的丈夫90岁，而我74岁，尽管我们在抵达时已经向机场工作人员告知了情况，但我们却被遗弃了！我们不知道如何到达酒店，因为我们已经预订了接送服务。我们至少向六位过往行人询问如何前往富拉玛酒店，每个人都非常乐于助人，甚至还帮我们提行李。虽然悉尼人是繁华都市的居民，但他们却有着淳朴的善良！”
hypo_1: 新西兰霍克湾的苏·凯来信写道："我要感谢所有好心人，他们帮助我和丈夫从机场顺利抵达悉尼中央商务区的酒店。我丈夫90岁，我74岁，尽管抵达时向接机向导表明了身份，我们却被遗忘了！由于原本安排了接送服务，我们完全不知如何前往富丽华酒店。至少向六位不同人士问路时，每个人都热情相助，甚至帮我们搬运行李。悉尼人或许有着大都市的世故，却怀揣着小城镇的淳朴善意！"
hypo_2: 新西兰霍克斯湾的苏·凯（Sue Kay）写道：“我要感谢所有好心人帮助我和丈夫从机场找到悉尼中央商务区的酒店。我丈夫90岁，我74岁，尽管我们到达机场时已经向导游表明了身份，但还是被落下了！由于我们已经安排了接送服务，我们不知道如何去酒店。我们至少向六个人问路去Furama酒店，每个人都非常热心，甚至帮我们拿行李。悉尼人可能是大城市里的精明人，但他们却有着小镇般的善良！”

source_doc: Some folks really do deserve a badge of honour for their pedantry (C8). Veronica Coyne of Springfield claims that "when bemoaning the loss of the express lane at Woolies "12 items or less," a friend told me she'd never used it on principle as it should have been "12 items or fewer.""
hypo_0: 有些人因为过于吹毛求疵，确实应该受到表扬（C8）。来自斯普林菲尔德的维罗妮卡·科因说，当她抱怨“伍尔沃斯超市取消了‘12件商品或更少’的快速通道”时，一位朋友告诉她，她一直坚持不用这个通道，因为她认为应该写成“12件商品或更少”。
hypo_1: 确实有些人因其吹毛求疵的精神而值得获得荣誉徽章（C8）。斯普林菲尔德的维罗妮卡·科因称：“当有人抱怨伍尔沃斯超市取消了‘12件商品或更少’的快速结账通道时，一位朋友告诉我，她从未使用过该通道，因为按照语法规则，正确的表述应该是‘12件商品或更少’（‘12 items or fewer’），而不是‘12件商品或以下’（‘12 items or less’）。”
hypo_2: 有些人确实应该获得荣誉徽章，以表彰他们对细节的执着（C8）。来自斯普林菲尔德的维罗尼卡·科恩声称，“当哀叹在沃尔玛失去‘12件或更少’的快速通道时，一位朋友告诉我她从原则上从未使用过这个通道，因为它应该被称为‘12件或更少’。”
hypo_3: 有些人确实因其吹毛求疵的态度而值得获得荣誉徽章（C8）。斯普林菲尔德的维罗妮卡·科因（Veronica Coyne）称：“当我抱怨Woolies的‘12件或更少’快速通道被取消时，一位朋友告诉我，她从未出于原则使用过它，因为标识应该是‘12件或更少’。”
hypo_4: 有些人确实因他们的吹毛求疵而值得获得一枚“荣誉徽章”（C8）。斯普林菲尔德的韦罗妮卡·科恩说：“当我抱怨伍尔沃斯超市‘12件或更少’快速结账通道消失时，一位朋友告诉我，她原则上从不使用它，因为应该是‘12件或更少’而不是‘12件或更少’。”
hypo_5: 有些人确实因为他们的刻板印象而应该获得荣誉徽章（C8）。斯普林菲尔德的维罗尼卡·科恩声称，“当我在为伍利斯超市‘12件或更少’的快捷通道的消失而哀叹时，一个朋友告诉我，她从原则上来说从未使用过这个通道，因为它应该是‘12件或更少’。”
hypo_6: 有些人确实因为他们的细心而值得获得荣誉徽章（C8）。斯普林菲尔德的维罗尼卡·科伊恩声称，“当我在哀叹伍利斯超市的快车道被取消时，一个朋友告诉我，她从来没有使用过快车道，因为按照原则，它应该是“12个或更少的物品”。”
hypo_7: 有些人确实因为他们的学究气而值得获得荣誉徽章（C8）。来自斯普林菲尔德的维罗妮卡·科因声称，"当抱怨伍尔沃斯快速通道'12件商品或更少'的消失时，一位朋友告诉我，她从来没有使用过这个通道，因为原则上它应该是'12件商品或更少'。"

source_doc: "Talk of badges reminds of the famous one from the 1960s-70s," says Merran Loewenthal of Birchgrove. ""It Begins When You Sink in His Arms and Ends with Your Arms in His Sink."" Jonty Grinter of Katoomba has one that reads "Chronically Advantaged" and adds that "it should be standard issue for all over seventy."
hypo_0: “说到徽章，让我想起了上世纪60至70年代那枚广为人知的徽章，”伯奇格罗夫（Birchgrove）的梅兰·洛文塔尔（Merran Loewenthal）说，“上面写着：‘爱情始于你投入他的怀抱，终于你双手浸入他家的洗碗池’。”卡图姆巴（Katoomba）的乔蒂·格林特（Jonty Grinter）拥有一枚写着“长期受益者”（Chronically Advantaged）的徽章，并补充道：“这应该成为所有年过七旬的老人的标配。”
hypo_1: "谈到徽章让我想起了1960-70年代的那个著名徽章，"来自伯奇格罗夫的梅兰·洛文塔尔说。"'当你沉浸在他的怀抱中时开始，当你的手臂在他的水槽中时结束。'"来自卡通巴的琼蒂·格林特有一个写着"长期优势者"的徽章，并补充说"这应该是所有70岁以上人群的标准配置。"
hypo_2: “谈到徽章，让我想起了20世纪60年代至70年代著名的徽章，”伯奇格罗夫的梅兰·洛文塔尔说。“它始于你沉浸在他的怀抱中，终于你的手臂沉浸在他的水槽里。”卡托姆巴的乔恩蒂·格林特有一个徽章，上面写着“长期受益者”，并补充说“这应该是所有70岁以上的人的标准配置。”
hypo_3: "说到勋章，让我想起上世纪六七十年代的经典段子，"桦树谷的梅兰·勒温塔尔说，"爱情始于你沉溺于他的臂弯，终于你的双手泡在他的洗碗池。"卡通巴的琼蒂·格林特有一枚写着"先天优势"的徽章，并补充道"这该成为所有七十岁以上人士的标准配饰"。
hypo_4: “说到徽章，就让我想起20世纪60至70年代那枚著名的徽章，”伯奇格罗夫的梅兰·洛文塔尔说。“上面写着：‘始于你沉入他的怀抱，终于你的双臂伸入他家的水槽。’”卡通巴的乔恩蒂·格林特也有一枚徽章，上面写着“长期受益者”，他补充说：“这应该成为所有七十岁以上老人的标配。”
hypo_5: Birchgrove 的 Merran Loewenthal 说：“说起徽章，让人想起 20 世纪 60 年代和 70 年代的著名徽章。”“它从你沉入他怀抱开始，到你的手臂在他的水槽里结束。”Katoomba 的 Jonty Grinter 有一枚上面写着“慢性优势”的徽章，并补充说：“它应该成为所有 70 岁以上人的标准装备。”
hypo_6: “提到徽章，让人想起20世纪60-70年代的那款著名徽章，”伯奇格罗夫的梅兰·洛文塔尔说，“它描述的是‘当你沉溺于他的怀抱时开始，以你的手臂在他水槽上结束’。”卡图姆巴的乔尼·格林特有一枚写着“长期优待（Chronically Advantaged）”的徽章，他补充说，“它应该成为所有70岁以上人士的标配。”
hypo_7: “谈到徽章，就让人想起 20 世纪 60 年代到 70 年代的那个著名的徽章，” Birchgrove 的 Merran Loewenthal 说道。“它从你沉浸在他的怀抱中开始，到你把手伸进他的水槽结束。”Katoomba 的 Jonty Grinter 有一枚上面写着“Chronically Advantaged”（长期处于优势地位）的徽章，并补充说，“这应该是所有 70 岁以上的人都应该佩戴的标准徽章。”

source_doc: As we haven't heard from Tony Nicod of Collaroy for nine months, one could consider this offering as a radical re-entry: "Finally the kids are back at school. No more grommets in the water taking waves off us retirees who like to surf "gentlemen's hours" from 8am."
hypo_0: 由于我们已经九个月没有收到科拉罗伊的托尼·尼科德（Tony Nicod）的来信，可以认为这封来信是一次激进的回归：“终于孩子们回学校了。再也没有小孩在水里抢走我们这些退休人士喜欢在上午8点‘绅士时间’冲浪的浪花了。”
hypo_1: 由于我们已经九个月没收到科拉罗伊的托尼·尼科德的消息了，可以认为这篇文章是一种激进的回归：“孩子们终于回学校了。不再有小毛头在水中抢我们的浪，我们这些喜欢在‘绅士时间’——早上8点——冲浪的退休人员可以享受冲浪乐趣了。”
hypo_2: 由于我们已经九个月没有收到来自 Collaroy 的 Tony Nicod 的消息，我们可以将这份作品视为他的一次激进回归：“孩子们终于又回到了学校。再也不用担心那些冲浪爱好者在水中冲浪，影响我们这些喜欢在上午 8 点以后‘绅士时段’冲浪的退休人士。”
hypo_3: 由于九个月来没有听到来自科拉罗伊的托尼·尼科德的消息，有人可能会认为这个提议是重新进入市场的激进举措：“孩子们终于回到学校了。再也不用在水中穿着耳塞，让我们这些喜欢在上午八点享受“绅士时光”冲浪的退休人员去应对海浪了。”
hypo_4: Collaroy 的 Tony Nicod 九个月来没有和我们取得联系，因此我们可以将他的这条推文视作一个激进的回归之举：“终于孩子们回到了学校。我们这些喜欢在”绅士时间“ (从早上 8 点开始) 冲浪的退休人士，再也不用让那些水边的小家伙们来制造浪花了！”

hypo_7: 科勒罗伊的托尼·尼科德已有九个月未曾来稿，此次投稿可谓是一次重磅回归：“孩子们终于回学校了。水中再没有冲浪小子和这些喜欢从早上8点享受‘绅士时段’冲浪的退休人士抢浪了。”

source_doc: Readers will take a shine to this item from Janice Creenaune of Austinmer: "Thanks for the wonderful memories Hendrik Kolenberg (C8). I kept my father's beautifully constructed wooden valet box. It remains filled with old Kiwi polish and brushes (some admittedly a little dried). He brushed my shoes every day of my school life to a high shine as he always thought it a sign of character. (My husband and I did maintain the task with our own children.) And the valet box remains well stocked, albeit sometimes with those little sponge squeeze bottles as well."
hypo_0: 读者们想必会对奥斯汀默的贾尼斯·克里诺恩发来的这则分享眼前一亮：“感谢亨德里克·科伦伯格（C8专栏）带来的美好回忆。我一直保存着父亲那个制作精美的木制梳理用具盒。里面仍然装满了旧的‘奇异’（Kiwi）牌鞋油和刷子（得承认有些已经干了）。在我整个学生时代，他每天都把我的鞋擦得锃亮，因为他一直认为这是品格的象征。（我和我丈夫也为我们的孩子坚持了这项任务。）而且这个梳理用具盒里依然储备充足，尽管有时也放着那些小小的海绵头挤压瓶。”

source_doc: I'm not sure I can remember exactly when I sensed it. Maybe it was when some of the more gimmicky London pubs introduced "stamp cards" to ration out Guinness after news of a shortage; maybe it was when I was served the 500th London Guinness rating video by my Instagram algorithm. Regardless of the precise moment the notion arrived, however, I have felt for a while that we are heading for a change when it comes to the pint du jour. For a few years now, Guinness has been the fashionable choice for millennial and gen Z drinkers, swept up in the aesthetic renaissance of the Good Old-Fashioned Bloody Pub. But I think the tide is turning. This will probably be the year that the Irish stout stops being cool.
hypo_0: 我不确定自己究竟是何时察觉到这一点的。也许是当一些伦敦的噱头十足的酒吧在传出供应短缺的消息后，开始推出“印章卡”来配给健力士啤酒时；也许是当我的Instagram算法为我推送了第500个伦敦健力士评分视频时。无论这个想法是在哪一刻产生的，我已经有一段时间感到，我们在当下流行的啤酒选择上即将迎来变化。几年来，健力士一直是千禧一代和Z世代饮酒者的时尚选择，他们被“传统老式酒吧”的美学复兴所吸引。但我认为潮流正在转变。今年可能是爱尔兰黑啤不再酷的一年。
hypo_1: 我不确定我能记得我什么时候意识到这一点。也许是在一些伦敦酒吧引入“邮票卡”来限量供应吉尼斯啤酒后，人们开始知道吉尼斯啤酒短缺；也许是在我通过 Instagram 算法收到第 500 个伦敦吉尼斯啤酒评价视频时。无论这个概念何时出现，我都感觉我们正在走向一场关于日常饮品的变革。几年来，吉尼斯啤酒一直是千禧一代和 Z 世代的时尚选择，他们被“老派血腥酒吧”的美学复兴所吸引。但我认为潮流正在转变。今年可能是爱尔兰黑啤酒不再时尚的年份。

source_doc: I'm pretty sure that what will finally usher the Guinness hype train back into the station will be the same thing that came for Aperol spritzes and truffle: overexposure. The Aperol, once a genuinely trendy, vaguely Sex and the City-ish aperitivo-style drink is now a cultural behemoth in its own right - delicious, certainly, but definitely no longer "cool." And truffle, well ... would you like a side of truffle fries for that truffle mac and cheese garnished with truffle oil?
hypo_0: 我敢肯定，最终让健力士的热度退潮的，将是和阿佩罗酒、松露一样的东西：过度曝光。阿佩罗曾是一种真正时髦的、带有《欲望都市》风格的开胃酒，如今已成为一种文化巨兽——当然，它依然美味，但绝对不再“酷”。至于松露……你想在松露奶酪通心粉上再加一份松露薯条，再淋上松露油吗？

source_doc: Overexposure, of course, is the death knell for cool in most cases (or at least self-conscious, trend-following "coolness," as I mean it here). This is particularly true amid the one-upmanship of contemporary food and drink culture which, as defined by social media, is always about being the first to find the undiscovered or most novel thing - the "hidden gem," the bakery and "pasta space" in London's zone 6 or what have you ("Come with me to the pub in Richmond that regularly floods"). And while Guinness's current round of popularity began because it was once viewed among younger pubgoers as a left-field choice - an "if you know, you know" order - it now couldn't be less of a secret.
hypo_0: 当然，过度曝光是大多数情况下“酷”的死敌（或者至少是自我意识强、跟风的“酷”，我这里指的是这种情况）。这在当代食品和饮料文化中尤其如此，根据社交媒体的定义，这种文化总是关于成为第一个发现未被发现或最新的东西——“隐藏的瑰宝”、伦敦第6区的面包店和“面食空间”等等（“让我们一起去 Richmond 的一家酒吧，那里经常淹水”）。而 Guinness 的当前流行周期之所以开始，是因为年轻的酒吧顾客曾经认为它是一个非主流选择——“如果你知道，你就知道”的命令，但现在它已经不再是秘密了。
hypo_1: 当然，过度曝光往往会使任何事物失去“酷”的魅力（或者说，失去那种追逐潮流、自命不凡的“酷”）。尤其是在当今的餐饮文化中，这种现象更为明显。在社交媒体的影响下，人们总是想成为第一个发现“隐藏的宝藏”、伦敦“第六区”的特色面包店或“意面店”，或者“去里士满那家经常被淹的酒吧”的人——总之，就是要走在潮流前沿。而吉尼斯啤酒之所以再次流行起来，最初是因为它曾被年轻的酒吧顾客视为一种“另类”选择，一种“懂的人自然懂”的饮品。但现在，它已经不再是什么秘密了。
hypo_2: 当然，在大多数情况下（至少就我所指的那种刻意追求潮流、标榜“酷”的行为而言），过度曝光恰恰是“酷感”消亡的开始。这一点在当代饮食文化中尤为明显，而这种文化正是由社交媒体所塑造的：人们总在争先恐后地寻找那些尚未被发掘、或是最新奇的事物——比如伦敦六区某家“隐秘”的面包店、某处“独具匠心”的意大利面餐厅等等（“跟我去里士满那家经常被水淹的酒吧吧”）。吉尼斯之所以能在年轻酒客中重新流行起来，最初正是因为被视为一种小众、不落俗套的选择——一种“懂的人自然懂”的饮品。但如今，它早已不再是“秘密”，反而变得司空见惯、毫无神秘感可言了。

source_doc: Undeniably, Guinness is everywhere. For the 2024-25 season, it shafted Budweiser to become the official beer of the Premier League, and will hold this honour for the next four years. It is the official beer of rugby's Six Nations tournament (meaning that for the next month, it is also the official beer of people called Henry). And a few months ago, news broke that the brand will be opening a Guinness Storehouse attraction, described as a "microbrewery and culture hub," in Covent Garden - despite the fact that London has nothing to do with Guinness or its 250-year-old heritage. Add to this the countless Hinge prompts about "splitting the G," and the impenetrable gaggle of gilets that pretty much always surrounds Soho's Guinness Disneyland, otherwise known as the wildly successful Devonshire pub, and you have to admit that the brand's cool stock is sinking.
hypo_0: 毫无疑问，健力士无处不在。在 2024-2025 赛季，它取代了百威成为英超联赛的官方啤酒，并将在接下来的四年里保持这一荣誉。它也是六国橄榄球锦标赛的官方啤酒（这意味着在接下来的一个月里，它也是亨利人的官方啤酒）。几个月前，有消息称，这家品牌将在考文特花园开设一座 Guinness Storehouse 景点，这座景点被描述为“微型酿酒厂和文化中心”——尽管伦敦与健力士或其 250 年的历史毫无关系。再加上无数关于“分割 G”的 Hinge 提示，以及围绕着 Soho 的 Guinness Disneyland（即广受欢迎的 Devonshire 酒吧）的难以穿透的吉尔特群，你不得不承认，这家品牌的酷炫形象正在逐渐消失。

source_doc: I don't speak as a Guinness naysayer. On the contrary, I grew up with a Dublin man and former pub landlord for a grandfather, so reverence for the stout is all but in my blood. As such, Guinness is pretty much the only pint I regularly drink, and I'll even admit that I'm One Of Those People who has long-winded opinions about their favourite Guinness in London, and who really does believe that it differs in quality depending on where you order it (it's all in the gas, the installation and the line-cleaning).
hypo_0: 我这么说，并非是健力士的反对者。恰恰相反，我的外祖父是一位都柏林人，也曾是酒吧老板，所以对这款黑啤的敬畏几乎融入了我的血液。因此，健力士几乎是我常喝的唯一一种啤酒，我甚至承认我就是那种人——对伦敦哪里的健力士最好喝有长篇大论，我真心认为，根据点酒地点的不同，这酒的品质也会有所差异（全在于气泡、设备安装和管道清洁）。
hypo_1: 我并不是要批评健力士啤酒。恰恰相反，我的祖父是都柏林人，而且曾是酒吧老板，从小我就耳濡目染，对这种黑啤酒充满了敬意，可以说，这种敬意已经深深地融入了我的血液。因此，健力士啤酒几乎是我唯一经常喝的啤酒，而且我甚至可以承认，我是那种对伦敦各家酒吧的健力士啤酒品质都有着自己独特见解的人，并且我真的相信，不同酒吧的健力士啤酒品质确实存在差异（这都取决于啤酒的气体含量、安装方式以及管道清洁情况）。

source_doc: While Guinness may be at significant risk of losing its edge among, say, people who queue outside bakeries, this hardly matters to its bottom line. It has surpassed cult status, and has in essence become too big to fail. Debra Crew, chief executive of Diageo, the drinks conglomerate that owns the brand, told press this week that demand for the stout in October and November 2024 had surpassed even the amount called for during St Patrick's Day celebrations, noting that the clamour was "unprecedented." Crew also stated that Diageo is spending €200m (£170m) on a new Guinness factory in Kildare to keep supplies up to the level now required.

source_doc: Music to my ears, really - I'm just pleased that while years ago you could walk into a pub and reasonably expect that Guinness wouldn't be served there, these days I can pretty much get my preferred pint anywhere. And, among all of the arguments to explain the surge in approval for the drink, it is important to acknowledge the obvious one: its smooth texture and subtly rich taste do just combine to offer something genuinely delicious, even when you get a mediocre one.
hypo_0: 这对我来说真是太好了——我很高兴，几年前，你走进一家酒吧，合理地期望那里不会供应健力士啤酒，而现在，我几乎可以在任何地方得到我喜欢的半升啤酒。而且，在解释这种饮料受欢迎程度激增的所有论点中，必须承认一个显而易见的事实：即使你得到了一杯平庸的啤酒，它的口感顺滑和微妙的浓郁味道也恰到好处地结合在一起，提供了一种真正美味的体验。

source_doc: Ultimately, I'm more than happy to let the hype move on to a less obvious drink (over the past couple of weeks, I've been in two south-east London pubs that have recently installed the rival stout Murphy's, owing to local demand - go figure). Because as annoying as social media Guinness chat might be, and despite the extent to which the Schooner Scorer's camera angles haunt my nightmares (I mean it, don't Google it), it's like a wise man called Robbie Williams once sang: "You can't argue with popularity. Well you could, but you'd be wrong."
hypo_0: 最终，我很乐意让炒作转向一种不那么显眼的饮料（在过去几周里，我去过两家东南伦敦的酒吧，它们最近因当地需求而引入了竞争对手的黒啤——墨菲（Murphy's）——你自己去想吧）。因为尽管社交媒体上的健力士讨论可能令人烦恼，尽管“Schooner Scorer”的摄像机角度在我梦中挥之不去（我说真的，别去谷歌搜索它），正如一位名叫罗比·威廉姆斯（Robbie Williams）的智者曾经唱过的：“你无法与流行争辩。你可以争辩，但你会错的。”
hypo_1: 归根结底，我非常乐意让热度转移到其他不那么显眼的酒品上（过去几周，我在伦敦东南部的两家酒吧发现它们因本地需求新上了竞争对手Murphy's黑啤——你品，你细品）。因为，尽管社交媒体上关于健力士的讨论令人厌烦，尽管Schooner Scorer的拍摄角度让我噩梦连连（我是认真的，别去搜），但正如一位名叫Robbie Williams的智者曾唱过的：“你无法与流行对抗。你可以试试，但你会错的。”
hypo_2: 归根结底，我很乐意让潮流转向另一种不那么显眼的饮品（在过去几周里，我去了两家位于伦敦东南部的酒吧，由于当地需求，它们最近都引进了竞争对手墨菲黑啤（Murphy's Stout）——你猜怎么着）。因为尽管社交媒体上关于吉尼斯的讨论可能令人厌烦，尽管“双桅帆船记分员”（Schooner Scorer）的拍摄角度在我的噩梦中挥之不去（我是认真的，别去谷歌搜索），就像一位名叫罗比·威廉姆斯（Robbie Williams）的智者曾经唱过的那样：“你无法与流行争辩。嗯，你可以争，但你会错。”
hypo_3: 说到底，我非常乐意看到这股热潮转向另一款更小众的饮品（过去几周，我去了伦敦东南部的两家酒吧，它们都因为当地的需求，最近引进了健力士的竞争对手——墨菲（Murphy's）世涛啤酒，真是有趣）。因为，尽管社交媒体上关于健力士的讨论可能很烦人，尽管“大杯评分家”（Schooner Scorer）的拍摄角度在我的噩梦中挥之不去（我是认真的，别去搜），但这就像一位名叫罗比·威廉姆斯的智者曾经唱过的那样：“你无法与流行争辩。好吧，你可以，但你错了。”
hypo_4: 总而言之，我很乐意让这种“炒作”转移到其他不太知名的酒品上（最近几周，我去了伦敦东南部的两家酒吧，它们都因为当地顾客的要求而开始供应竞争对手的黑啤酒“墨菲”，真是令人惊讶）。虽然社交媒体上关于健力士啤酒的讨论可能会让人感到厌烦，而且“斯库纳得分”的拍摄角度也常常让我做噩梦（我说的是真的，千万不要去搜索它），但正如一位名叫罗比·威廉姆斯的智者曾经唱过的：“你无法反驳流行，就算你反驳，你也一定是错的。”
hypo_5: 最终，我非常乐意让热度转向一种不那么显眼的饮品（在过去的几周里，我去过两家东南伦敦的酒吧，它们最近因当地需求安装了竞品黑啤墨菲——想想看）。因为无论社交媒体上关于健力士的讨论有多烦人，尽管“Schooner Scorer”的拍摄角度让我做噩梦（我是说真的，别去搜），但正如一位名叫罗比·威廉姆斯的智者曾经唱过的那样：“你无法与流行争辩。当然，你可以争辩，但你会是错的。”
hypo_6: 归根结底，我非常乐意让这股热潮转向一种不那么大众的饮品（在过去几周里，我光顾的两家伦敦东南部的酒吧都因当地需求而新安装了其竞争对手墨菲黑啤的设备——谁能想到呢）。因为，尽管社交媒体上关于健力士的讨论可能很烦人，尽管那些啤酒评分博主的拍摄角度在我的噩梦中挥之不去（我是认真的，别去搜），但正如一位名叫罗比·威廉姆斯的智者曾经唱过的那样：“你无法与流行争辩。好吧，你可以争，但那样你就错了。”
hypo_7: 最终，我很乐意看营销热潮转向其他低调饮品（最近因本地需求，东南伦敦两家酒吧竟引进了竞品墨菲黑啤——世事难料）。因为无论社交媒体上的健力士话题多烦人，无论"酒杯评分师"的拍摄角度如何成为我的噩梦（真的，别去搜），正如智者罗比·威廉姆斯所唱："你无法与流行抗衡。当然可以尝试，但必败无疑。"

source_doc: In these days of darkness we must take delight where we can find it, and one reliable source is the Duchess of Sussex. In her latest venture, Meghan, who occupies the tricky position of having a large platform and not enough to fill it (see also: Brooklyn Beckham), launched a new lifestyle brand that, she promised in a short video, would be "beautifully weaving together everything I cherish - food, gardening, entertaining, thoughtful living and finding joy in the everyday." The brand American Riviera Orchard is dead. Long live the new brand, As Ever.
hypo_0: 在这个黑暗的时代，我们要尽可能地去寻找快乐。而一位可靠的快乐提供者，就是萨塞克斯公爵夫人。身处平台大而没有足够东西可以填充的尴尬地位（请参见布鲁克林·贝克汉姆）的梅根，推出了一款新的生活品牌。她在一段短视频中承诺：这款品牌将“美丽地将我珍视的所有东西融合在一起——食物、园艺、社交、生活哲理、以及对日常生活的细腻感悟”。这个品牌叫做“美国里维埃拉果园”（American Riviera Orchard），这个老品牌已经消失了，现在这个新品牌“永远美好”（As Ever）就要崛起了。
hypo_1: 在这个黑暗的时代，我们必须在能找到喜乐的地方寻得慰藉，而一个可靠的来源就是苏塞克斯公爵夫人。在她最新的尝试中，梅根（她处于一个微妙的位置：拥有一个广阔的平台，但内容却不足以填满这个平台，另见：布鲁克林·贝克汉姆）推出了一种新的生活方式品牌，她在一个短片中承诺，这个品牌将“美妙地融合我所珍惜的一切——美食、园艺、娱乐、深思熟虑的生活和在日常生活中寻找快乐。”品牌American Riviera Orchard已不复存在。愿新品牌As Ever长盛不衰。
hypo_2: 在这个充满阴霾的时代，我们必须努力从生活中寻找乐趣，而苏塞克斯公爵夫人无疑是一个可靠的快乐源泉。在她的最新创业项目中，梅根推出了一个全新的生活方式品牌。她所处的处境颇为尴尬：虽然拥有巨大的平台，却苦于缺乏足够内容来填充这个平台（参见：布鲁克林·贝克汉姆）。她在一段简短的视频中承诺，这个新品牌将会“巧妙地融合我所珍视的一切——美食、园艺、娱乐、用心生活以及在日常生活中发现快乐”。旧品牌“美国里维埃拉果园”（American Riviera Orchard）已经寿终正寝，新品牌“As Ever”正式登场。
hypo_3: 在这个黯淡的时代，我们必须在能寻得欢愉处及时行乐，而苏塞克斯公爵夫人总是不负所望。这位坐拥巨大平台却苦于内容匮乏（参见：布鲁克林·贝克汉姆）的女士，在其最新商业企划中推出全新生活方式品牌。她通过短视频承诺，该品牌将"美妙地融合我所珍视的一切——美食、园艺、娱乐、深思熟虑的生活态度，以及发现日常中的喜悦"。美国里维埃拉果园品牌已死，新品牌"如初"万岁。
hypo_4: 在这些黑暗的日子里，我们必须尽力寻找乐趣，而杜克萨斯郡主是一个可靠的信息源。在她最新的尝试中，梅根——她处于拥有很大平台却没有足够内容来填充的尴尬境地（参见：布鲁克林·贝克汉姆）——推出了一个新的生活方式品牌，并在一段短视频中承诺，这个品牌将“把我珍视的一切——食物、园艺、娱乐、体贴的生活和在日常生活中寻找快乐——美丽地交织在一起。”美国里维埃拉果园这个品牌已经寿终正寝。新的品牌“如常”（As Ever）万岁。

hypo_6: 在这个黑暗的时代，我们必须在力所能及之处寻找快乐，而苏塞克斯公爵夫人就是这样一个可靠来源。在她的最新项目中，拥有庞大平台却内容匮乏（参见：布鲁克林·贝克汉姆）的梅根，通过一段短视频承诺推出一个全新的生活方式品牌，称其将“优美地融合我所珍视的一切——美食、园艺、社交、用心生活以及在日常中发现快乐。”美国河畔果园品牌已死，新品牌“一如既往（As Ever）”万岁。
hypo_7: 在这黑暗的日子里，我们必须抓住一切可以带来快乐的机会，而梅根王妃无疑是可靠的来源之一。 在她最新的尝试中，梅根面临着拥有广泛影响力的平台却难以充分利用的挑战（例如：布鲁克林·贝克汉姆），她推出了一项新的生活方式品牌，并承诺在一段简短的视频中，该品牌将汇集她珍视的一切——美食、园艺、娱乐、深思熟虑的生活以及在日常生活中发现快乐。 品牌“美国里维埃拉果园”已停止运营。 欢迎新品牌“As Ever”。

source_doc: The duchess and her husband, Prince Harry, have to do something and good luck to them, but it's hard not to study the rollout of As Ever for signs of the widening gap between Meghan's self-image and how the rest of the world sees her. ""As ever,"" writes the duchess on Instagram, "means "as it's always been" or some even say "in the same way as always,"" the "some" in this sentence apparently referring to the dictionary definition of a two-word phrase that no one has ever had trouble understanding.
hypo_0: 公爵夫人和她的丈夫哈里王子总得做点什么，祝他们好运，但人们很难不去审视 As Ever 的推出过程，并从中寻找梅根的自我形象与外界对她的看法之间日益扩大的鸿沟的迹象。公爵夫人在 Instagram 上写道：“As ever 的意思是‘一如既往’，或者有人说‘和往常一样’。”这句中的“有人”显然指的是词典对这个无人不解的两词短语的定义。
hypo_1: 公爵夫妇总得谋些营生，我们理应祝他们好运。但审视"如初"的发布过程，很难不注意到梅根自我认知与外界观感间的鸿沟正日益扩大。"'如初'意味着'一如往昔'，也有人说是'始终如一'"，公爵夫人在Instagram写道。此处的"有人"显然是指词典对这个人尽皆知的短语的定义。
hypo_2: 公爵夫人和她的丈夫哈里王子必须有所作为，祝他们好运，但很难不在As Ever的发布过程中寻找梅根自我形象与外界看法之间日益扩大的差距。“As ever，”公爵夫人在Instagram上写道，“意思是‘一如既往’，或者有人说‘始终如一’。”这句话中的“有人”显然指的是这个两词短语的字典定义，而这个短语从未让任何人感到困惑。
hypo_3: 公爵夫人和她的丈夫哈里王子必须做点什么，祝他们好运，但很难不研究一下“As Ever”的推出，以便了解梅根的自我形象与世界其他地方对她的看法之间的差距。公爵夫人在 Instagram 上写道：“As ever” 意味着“一直如同从前”，有些人甚至说“以往一直如此”，”这句话中的“有些人”显然是指字典中定义的两字短语，没有人会有理解上的困难。

hypo_5: 公爵夫人和她的丈夫哈里王子面临着挑战，祝他们好运。但很难不关注“As Ever”的发布，从中观察梅根的自我形象与外界看法之间的差距。公爵夫人写道，她在Instagram上解释，“As ever”的意思是“一如既往”，或有人说“始终如一”。这句话中的“有人”显然指代一个双字短语的字典含义，这一直以来都很好理解。

source_doc: It's ridiculous, of course, as is the hello-little-people tone adopted by the former royal highness, in keeping with the general attitude of the famous towards the unfamous - namely, one of stating the bleeding obvious as though sharing the key to existence. Still, I find myself sympathising with Meghan, who in her Instagram post and its accompanying video faces the stark challenge of having to pad out thin material. "This is what I do," she says to camera, "and I haven't been able to share it with you for the past few years, but now I can." What is the "it" here, and what has stopped her from sharing it? (It may involve jam, as did her previous brand, which she launched by sending "limited edition" jars of preserve to, among others, Kris Jenner, Mindy Kaling and Tracee Ellis Ross, who apparently also got a fresh bag of lemons.) Anyway, whatever the content, she hints darkly at some "whispers" abroad that forced her to jump the gun and share news of As Ever before she was ready - because "I wanted you to hear it from me first."
hypo_0: 这当然是荒谬的，就如这位前王室贵妇所采用的“你好，小老百姓”的语气一样，这符合名人对非名人的一般态度——即陈述显而易见的事实，仿佛在分享生存的关键。然而，我发现自己对梅根抱有同情，她在Instagram帖子和配套视频中面临着严峻挑战，不得不用稀薄的内容来充数。“这就是我所做的事，”她对着镜头说，“在过去的几年里我一直没能与你们分享，但现在我可以了。”这里的“它”指的是什么，是什么阻止了她分享它？（这可能涉及果酱，正如她之前的品牌一样，她通过向克里斯·詹娜、敏迪·卡灵和特雷西·埃利斯·罗斯等人发送“限量版”果酱罐来推出该品牌，这些人显然还收到了一袋新鲜的柠檬。）无论内容如何，她暗暗暗示，国外的某些“耳语”迫使她在没有准备好的情况下提前分享了“As Ever”的消息——因为“我希望你们首先从我这里听到这个消息”。
hypo_1: 当然，这很荒谬，就像这位前皇室成员采用的“小人物你好”的语气一样，这与名人对普通人的普遍态度一致——即将显而易见的事情说得像是分享存在的关键。尽管如此，我发现自己同情梅根，她在Instagram帖子和配套视频中面临着一个严峻的挑战，即不得不填充稀薄的内容。“这就是我所做的”，她对着镜头说，“过去几年我无法与你们分享，但现在可以了。”这里的“它”是什么，又是什么阻止了她分享？（可能涉及果酱，就像她之前的品牌一样，她通过向克里斯·詹纳、明迪·卡林和特蕾西·埃利斯·罗斯等人寄送“限量版”果酱罐来推出该品牌，后者显然还收到了一袋新鲜柠檬。）无论如何，无论内容是什么，她暗示有些“流言”迫使她抢先分享“如昔”的消息，因为“我希望你们首先从我这里听到”。
hypo_2: 当然，这很荒谬，正如这位前皇室成员所采用的“嗨，小民们”的语气一样，这与名人对普通人的普遍态度一致，即把再明显不过的事情当作生存秘诀来分享。尽管如此，我还是对梅根产生了一丝同情，她在Instagram帖子及其配套视频中，面临着内容贫乏却要硬撑场面的严峻挑战。“这就是我所做的，”她对着镜头说，“过去几年我无法与你们分享，但现在可以了。”这里的“它”到底是什么？又是什么阻止了她分享？（这可能与果酱有关，因为她的上一个品牌就是通过向包括克丽丝·詹纳、敏迪·卡灵和特蕾丝·艾利斯·罗斯等人寄送“限量版”果酱罐而推出的，后者显然还收到了一袋新鲜柠檬。）无论如何，不管内容是什么，她暗示有某些“流言”在国外流传，迫使她提前公布“As Ever”的消息——因为“我想让你们第一个从我这里听到。”
hypo_3: 这当然是荒谬的，正如前王室成员所采用的“你好，小人物”的语气一样，这与名人对普通人的普遍态度相符——即把显而易见的事情说得好像是在分享存在的核心秘密。然而，我还是对梅根表示同情，她在Instagram帖子和随附的视频中面临着将稀薄的内容填充起来的严峻挑战。“这就是我所做的事情，”她对着镜头说，“过去几年我一直无法与你们分享，但现在我可以了。”这里的“它”指的是什么？是什么阻止了她分享？（这可能与果酱有关，就像她之前推出的品牌一样，她向包括克里斯·詹纳、明迪·卡林和特蕾西·埃利斯·罗斯在内的其他人寄送了“限量版”的果酱罐，他们似乎还收到了一袋新鲜的柠檬。）总之，无论内容是什么，她暗示着一些“耳语”迫使她提前分享了“As Ever”的消息——因为“我想让你们首先从我这里听到”。
hypo_4: 这当然很荒谬，前王室殿下采用的那种居高临下的语调也是如此，这与名人对非名人的普遍态度一致——即把显而易见的事情说得好像在分享生存的秘诀。不过，我发现自己同情梅根，她在Instagram帖子及其配套视频中面临着必须充实单薄材料的严峻挑战。"这就是我所做的，"她对着镜头说，"过去几年我一直无法与你们分享，但现在我可以了。"这里的"它"是什么，是什么阻止了她分享？（这可能涉及果酱，就像她之前的品牌一样，她通过向克里斯·詹纳、明迪·卡林和特蕾西·埃利斯·罗斯等人发送"限量版"果酱罐来推出该品牌，后者显然还收到了一袋新鲜柠檬。）无论如何，无论内容是什么，她暗示有一些"传言"迫使她匆忙行事，在准备好之前就分享"一如既往"的消息——因为"我希望你们首先从我这里听到。"
hypo_5: 当然，这有点荒谬，就像这位前王室成员所采用的那种“您好，小人物们”的调门一样，与公众人物对待普通人的普遍态度一样，也就是用通俗易懂的话来探讨存在的奥秘。我发现自己对梅根比较同情。在她发布到 Instagram 的帖子及其配套的视频中，她面临着一个严峻的挑战，那就是要让自己的“薄弱内容”看起来强大。她对镜头说：“这是我现在做的事情。过去几年，我一直没有机会与你分享我的这些工作，不过现在我可以了。”这里的“这”指的是什么？为什么她在过去几年没有与外界分享她的作品呢？（这里可能会有果酱，就像她之前的品牌一样，她向包括 Kris Jenner、Mindy Kaling 和 Tracee Ellis Ross 等在内的好友发了一些“限量版”果酱罐头，据说这些好友也有一些新鲜的柠檬。）无论内容是什么，她都暗示有“风言风语”传出，导致她不得不提前分享 As Ever 品牌的新闻，因为“我希望你们直接从我这里听到这些”。
hypo_6: 当然，这很荒谬，就像这位前王室成员所采用的那种对普通人采取的“你好，小人物”的语气一样，这与名人在对待非名人的时候的一贯态度一致——即，把显而易见的事实说成是分享存在的关键。尽管如此，我还是对梅根表示同情，她在Instagram帖子及其配套视频中面临着必须充实稀薄内容的严峻挑战。“这就是我所做的事情，”她对镜头说，“在过去的几年里，我无法与你们分享，但现在我可以。”这里的“它”是什么，什么阻碍了她与大家分享呢？（可能涉及果酱，就像她之前推出的品牌一样，她通过向克里斯·詹娜、敏迪·卡林和特蕾西·埃利斯·罗斯等送去“限量版”的果酱罐而推出了这个品牌，她们显然也收到了新鲜的柠檬。）无论内容如何，她暗指国外的一些“流言”迫使她在准备好之前就提前分享了As Ever的消息——因为“我想让你先从我这里听到这个消息。”
hypo_7: 这当然很荒谬，就像这位前殿下所采用的“你好，小人物们”的语气一样，这与名人对无名之辈的普遍态度一致——即把显而易见的事情说得仿佛是在分享人生的奥秘。尽管如此，我还是对梅根产生了同情。在她的Instagram帖子及配套视频中，她不得不面对用有限内容填充平台的严峻挑战。“这就是我所做的事情，”她对着镜头说，“过去几年我无法与你们分享，但现在我可以了。”这里的“这”指的是什么？又是什么阻止了她分享？（可能涉及果酱，就像她之前的品牌一样，她曾向克里斯·詹娜、敏迪·卡灵和特雷西·埃利斯·罗斯等人寄送“限量版”果酱，据说她们还收到了新鲜柠檬。）无论内容如何，她神秘地暗示有一些“流言”在外，迫使她提前公布As Ever的消息——因为“我希望你们首先从我这里听到。”

source_doc: The fact there is no story here makes Meghan's insistence that she is getting in front of the story all the more enjoyable, but the more curious aspect of the announcement is the "you" to whom she refers and the idea she clearly has of her audience. The celebrity lifestyle space is crowded, ferocious and notoriously fickle, littered with the carcasses of former brand leaders such as Chrissy Teigen. Target and Bloomingdales distanced themselves from the star around the same time as she was accused of, and later apologised for, cyberbullying a woman called Courtney. The space is also dominated by market leaders such as Gwyneth Paltrow and Rachel Zoe.

source_doc: Meghan is a minnow in these waters, but of course has her own secret weapon she isn't shy about using. In the new video, she deploys good old Harry to hold up the cameraphone and drop a few posh tones - "Is this thing on?" - to remind us that, even if As Ever isn't royal, exactly, it is at least royal adjacent. The new name is clearly a reach for something classier and more timeless than its predecessor, American Riviera Orchard, which sounded like a range of scented gift baskets you'd find at a discount shopping outlet, or the pair's charity arm, Archewell, a play on their son's name. Both brands have an aspirant lower middle-class vibe about them, which it would be rude to point out were it not for the fact that Harry and Meghan trade on class as part of their branding.
hypo_0: 在这些水域中，梅根只是一条小鱼，但她当然也有自己毫不避讳使用的秘密武器。在新视频中，她派出了我们亲爱的哈里，让他举着手机摄像头，说几句贵族腔调的话——“这玩意儿开着吗？”——来提醒我们，即使 As Ever 算不上真正的王室品牌，也至少是“王室周边”。新名字显然是为了追求比其前身更经典、更永恒的东西，比如 American Riviera Orchard，它听起来像是在折扣购物中心能找到的香氛礼品篮系列；或是他们夫妇的慈善机构 Archewell——这个名字取自他们儿子的名字。这两个品牌都带有一种渴望向上爬的下层中产阶级的气息，如果不是因为哈里和梅根将阶级作为其品牌的一部分进行交易，指出这一点或许会显得无礼。

source_doc: What they are selling isn't poshness itself, which strictly speaking should entail low-comfort, hole-in-the-elbows, bad cooking and draughty houses, but - much like Trump and his performance of "billionaire" - a kind of ersatz poshness they imagine might appeal to the peasants. It's a hard one to get right and, with the new brand name, Meghan seems to have changed course in the direction of, say, a Calvin Klein perfume or something a bit more abstract and upmarket. Although for my money she was on to more of a winner with the obscure and personalised brand name The Tig, her first attempt at lifestyle content that had to be shelved when she started dating Harry. As she explained at the time, The Tig was derived from an abbreviation of her favourite wine, tignanello, and had about it the confidence of something genuinely felt rather than a brand name that has been kicked through a comms team.
hypo_0: 他们真正兜售的并非真正的贵族气息——严格来说，那应该意味着低舒适度、肘部有洞的衣服、糟糕的烹饪和漏风的房子——而更像特朗普表演“亿万富翁”那样，是一种他们想象中可能吸引平民的伪贵族气息。要准确拿捏这一点并不容易，而新品牌名称似乎正朝着卡尔文·克莱恩（Calvin Klein）香水或更抽象、更高档的方向转变。不过，以我的观点，她此前那个晦涩而个性化的品牌名“The Tig”其实更有潜力，那是她最初尝试生活方式内容时所起的名字，后来因与哈里开始交往而不得不搁置。当时她解释说，“The Tig”源自她最喜欢的葡萄酒“Tignanello”的缩写，这个名字带着一种真实情感的自信，而不是经过公关团队反复推敲出来的品牌名称。
hypo_1: 他们兜售的并非真正意义上的“贵族生活”（严格来说，真正的贵族生活意味着低舒适度、破旧的衣服、糟糕的食物和四处漏风的房子），而是像特朗普刻意营造的“亿万富翁”形象那样，一种他们想象中可能会吸引普通民众的“伪贵族生活”。这种定位其实很难拿捏得恰到好处。从新品牌名称来看，梅根似乎已经改变了方向，转向了类似卡尔文·克莱恩（Calvin Klein）香水那样更抽象、更高档的产品方向。不过依我之见，她最初使用的那个独特且个性化的品牌名称“The Tig”或许更胜一筹。那是她在与哈里王子交往前的一次尝试。正如她当时所解释的，“The Tig”这个名字来源于她最喜爱的葡萄酒“天娜”（Tignanello）的缩写，这个名字显得更加真实、自然，而非经过公关团队反复推敲出来的品牌名称。
hypo_2: 他们所售卖的并非真正的高贵生活方式，严格来说那应当是低舒适度、肘部磨破的衣服、糟糕的厨艺和漏风的房子，而是——就像特朗普表演“亿万富翁”那样——一种他们想象中可能吸引“平民”的伪高贵生活方式。这很难把握，而梅根似乎通过新品牌名称转向了类似Calvin Klein香水那样更抽象、更高端的路线。虽然在我看来，她最初的The Tig品牌名更具个性和成功潜力，那是她第一次尝试生活方式内容，但在与哈里约会后不得不搁置。正如她当时解释的，The Tig源自她最喜欢的葡萄酒tignanello的缩写，带有一种真正发自内心的自信，而不是经过公关团队反复推敲的品牌名。
hypo_3: 他们所出售的不是高雅本身——严格来说，高雅应该意味着低舒适度、肘部有洞、糟糕的烹饪和通风的房子——而是类似于特朗普和他表演的“亿万富翁”——一种他们想象中可能吸引普通人的仿冒高雅。这很难做到正确，而梅根似乎已经改变了方向，比如说，转向了卡尔文·克莱恩香水或一些更抽象、更高档的东西。虽然在我看来，她最初尝试的生活方式内容品牌“The Tig”更具潜力，那是一个在她与哈里王子约会时不得不搁置的品牌。正如她当时解释的那样，The Tig源自她最喜欢的葡萄酒——蒂尼亚内罗（tignanello）的缩写，并且具有真正被感受到的信心，而不是一个经过公关团队推敲的品牌名称。

source_doc: Which brings us to the duchess's understanding of audience. Discounting the forfeiture of sympathy that comes with money and privilege, there is something akin to pathos in the video that I find painful to watch. Meghan's aw-shucks, I-know-you-love-me tone assumes not only goodwill but abject fandom on the part of an audience which, I suspect, is at least 50% made up of those who regard her not only critically but with downright derision. It is hard to witness. "Sending lots of love," she signs off with noblesse oblige and an almost charming dedication to what she imagines we think of her.
hypo_0: 这让我们回到公爵夫人的观众理解问题上。撇开金钱和特权带来的同情心丧失不谈，我发现视频中有一种近似于悲情的氛围，让人感到痛苦。梅根那种“哎呀，我知道你们爱我”的语气，不仅假设了善意，还假设了观众对她的狂热崇拜，而我怀疑至少有50%的观众不仅对她持批判态度，甚至对她充满嘲讽。这很难看下去。“送上满满的爱”，她以一种贵族式的义务和近乎迷人的奉献精神结束了这段话，表达了她想象中我们对她的看法。

source_doc: It's not a Nordic culinary microtrend or a sex thing, but a beauty treatment, in which "Polydeoxyribonucleotides (PDRN) derived from purified fragments of DNA extracted from yes, "salmon sperm"" are injected into your face. It does seem to work: proper research has found PDRN has wound-repairing properties in hard-to-treat contexts such as diabetic ulcers and deep burns. Good news (unless you're a male salmon). But what is it doing in healthy faces?

source_doc: I learned about salmon sperm in a Sunday Times article on the "skincare secrets" of "26 tastemakers," which I read, increasingly aghast. It was a litany of lasers, microneedling, injectables and proprietary treatments with silly names that left me shouting crossly at my laptop: "But what does it do?" The only treatment I related to at all was Marina Abramović recounting how a friend of her mother's put hot mashed potato on her face to temporarily erase wrinkles; Abramović herself uses "thermage radiofrequency." No wonder a salmon sperm practitioner says it's "an exciting time in the aesthetic industry."
hypo_0: 我是在《星期日泰晤士报》一篇关于“26位潮流引领者‘护肤秘诀’”的文章中了解到鲑鱼精子的，读着读着我越来越震惊。那是一连串的激光、微针、注射和各种带着奇怪名字的专有疗法，让我气愤地对着笔记本电脑大喊：“但它到底有什么用？”唯一让我产生共鸣的疗法，是玛丽娜·阿布拉莫维奇回忆她母亲的朋友曾用热土豆泥敷脸，暂时消除皱纹；而阿布拉莫维奇本人则使用“热玛吉射频”。难怪一位鲑鱼精子疗法的从业者会说，现在是“美学行业令人兴奋的时代”。

source_doc: Some contributors were celebs, regularly running the unforgiving gauntlet of high-definition television, and the rest were at least very interested, if not professionally involved, in beauty, so they probably aren't typical. And there's a certain honesty to it, at least. No one is making out their dewy glow is "just good genes" and "loads of water." But I also think this newfound willingness to talk about "tweakments" (a jaunty portmanteau that makes me queasy) is a product of them being absolutely everywhere.
hypo_0: 某些分享者是名人，他们经常要经受高清电视毫不留情的考验，其余的人即使没有专业从事美容行业，也至少对此非常感兴趣，所以他们可能不具代表性。不过，这至少有一定的诚实度。没有人声称他们水润的光泽“只是基因好”和“大量喝水”的结果。但我也认为，这种新出现的谈论“微整形”（tweakments，一个让我感到恶心的轻快合成词）的意愿，正是它们已经无处不在而带来的产物。

source_doc: Because they are everywhere, for everyone. According to researchers at University College London, the UK injectables market will be worth £11.7bn by next year, with Botox and fillers available anywhere from Harley Street to high-street hairdressers. Writing in Grazia last week, the Guardian beauty columnist Sali Hughes described how "women in teaching, policing and the civil service" ask her advice on where to get "good injections." In the US, Botox use by 20- to 29-year-olds has increased 28% since 2010, with gen Z buying into "prejuvenation" (another awful portmanteau), fuelled by the poreless perfection offered by filters, staring at themselves on pandemic screens and social media skinfluencers (argh).
hypo_0: 因为它们确实无处不在，适用于所有人。根据伦敦大学学院的研究人员预测，到明年，英国注射类美容市场的价值将达到117亿英镑，从哈雷街到普通美发店都能做肉毒素和填充剂。上周在《Grazia》杂志上，《卫报》美容专栏作家萨莉·休斯描述了“从事教学、警务和公务员工作的女性”向她咨询哪里能做“靠谱的注射”。在美国，20至29岁人群使用肉毒素的比例自2010年以来增长了28%，Z世代也开始追捧“预防性抗衰老”（又一个令人不适的合成词），这背后是滤镜带来的无瑕皮肤、疫情期间长时间盯着屏幕以及社交媒体上的“护肤达人”共同推动的结果（唉）。

source_doc: I shouldn't be surprised - actual children are buying expensive anti-ageing potions and fretting about their nonexistent crow's feet now. But still, this dramatic normalisation of invasive beauty procedures is unnerving.

source_doc: For one thing, tweakments are so expensive! It's none of my business how people spend their money - my disposable income is dedicated to becoming the Joe Exotic of decorative poultry - but will we end up with an attractiveness inequality gap? Or will the democratisation of HD face mean almost everyone will be smoothly immobile soon? I suspect there will always be better and worse tweakments: that UCL research also points out how alarmingly unregulated the industry is.

source_doc: It's also uncomfortable feeling so out of step. My only foray into tweakment territory is getting my brows, lost to alopecia, tattooed back on (which demonstrates how central frowning is to my character, so Botox is out). It's not that I'm smugly delighted with my 50-year-old face: the baleful crone in my new passport photo appears, inexplicably, to have a single black eye (actually just a dark circle), adding to the "pensioner arrested after brawl outside bookies" vibe. It would be nice to feel better about my neck, but not nice enough to actually do anything painful and expensive about it.

source_doc: I suppose we tweakment holdouts will have to wait, and hope, for the tide to turn. As Ozempic and shiny veneers may make thinness and Hollywood teeth look boring and basic, perhaps perfect faces will fall from fashion? I mention the veneers, because when I was watching the Bridget Jones movie, I become fascinated by Chiwetel Ejiofor's teeth. They're absolutely lovely, yes, but sort of ... normal? One lower one peeps out from slightly behind the others. It only added to his charm. Hugh Grant's 64-year-old face looked charismatically crumpled too. Maybe when everyone has been homogenised to a glazed sheen of perfection, sagging, bumps, gaping pores and rough patches will be the height of desirability. At that point, I'll be ready for my closeup.
hypo_0: 我想那些坚持不懈的人们将不得不等待，并希望，形势好转。随着 Ozempic 和闪亮牙套可能使苗条和好莱坞牙齿看起来枯燥乏味，也许完美的面孔将不再流行？我提到牙套，是因为当我观看布丽吉特·琼斯的电影时，我对奇维特·埃吉奥福的牙齿着迷。它们绝对美丽，是的，但有点……正常？其中一颗下牙稍微从其他牙齿后面露出来。这只是增加了他的魅力。休·格兰特的 64 岁面孔看起来也很有魅力地皱巴巴的。也许当每个人都被同质化成完美的釉面时，下垂、凸起、大孔和粗糙的斑块将成为最受欢迎的特征。到那时，我将准备好我的特写镜头。
hypo_1: 我想，我们这些抗拒微调的人只能等待并希望潮流转向。也许当奥зем匹克（Ozempic）和闪亮的牙贴面让瘦身和好莱坞般的牙齿看起来枯燥无味时，完美的面容就会过时？我提到牙贴面，是因为我在看《布里奇特·琼斯》电影时，对丘维特尔·艾吉福（Chiwetel Ejiofor）的牙齿产生了兴趣。它们绝对可爱，但有点……普通？其中一颗下牙从其他牙齿后面微微露出来。这反而增加了他的魅力。休·格兰特64岁的脸也看起来很有魅力，布满皱纹。也许当每个人都被同质化到完美的玻璃光泽时，下垂、凸起、张开的毛孔和粗糙的皮肤就会成为令人渴望的。那时，我就准备好迎接我的特写镜头了。
hypo_2: 我想，我们这些坚持自然容貌、抵制微整形的人只能静待潮流转变。当减肥药“奥泽匹克”（Ozempic）和闪亮的牙贴面让纤瘦身材与“好莱坞式”完美牙齿变得司空见惯、甚至显得乏味时，也许“完美无瑕”的脸庞终将过时？我之所以提到牙贴面，是因为在观看《布里奇特·琼斯的日记》时，我被切瓦特·埃加福（Chiwetel Ejiofor）的牙齿深深吸引。他的牙齿当然非常漂亮，但并非那种“完美对称”的类型——下排有一颗牙齿微微错位，从其他牙齿后方探出一点点。这种“不完美”反而增添了他的个人魅力。休·格兰特那张64岁的脸，布满皱纹却依然充满魅力。也许当所有人都被“打造”成完美无瑕、泛着光泽的“蜡像”时，松弛的肌肤、自然的皱纹、粗大的毛孔以及不规则的肤质反而会成为最令人向往的特质。到那时，我便准备好迎接属于我的特写镜头了。
hypo_3: 我想我们这些微调美容的坚持者将不得不等待，并希望潮流会转向。正如奥泽匹克和闪亮贴面可能会让瘦身和好莱坞牙齿看起来无聊和基础，也许完美的面孔会失宠？我提到贴面，是因为当我看《布里奇特·琼斯》电影时，我对切瓦特·埃加福特的牙齿着迷了。它们绝对可爱，是的，但有点...正常？一颗下牙从其他牙齿后面稍微露出来。这只是增加了他的魅力。休·格兰特64岁的脸看起来也很有魅力地皱巴巴。也许当每个人都被同质化为完美的釉面光泽时，下垂、凸起、张开的毛孔和粗糙斑块将成为最令人向往的。到那时，我就准备好接受特写了。
hypo_4: 我想，我们这些拒绝微调的人只能等待并希望潮流能逆转。正如Ozempic（一种减肥药）和闪亮贴面牙可能让苗条身材和好莱坞式牙齿变得平淡无奇一样，完美的面容或许也会退出时尚潮流？我之所以提到贴面牙，是因为当我观看《布里吉特·琼斯》电影时，我被切瓦特·埃加福特（Chiwetel Ejiofor）的牙齿迷住了。它们确实非常漂亮，但看起来有点“普通”？其中一颗下牙稍微从其他牙齿后面探出了一点。但这反而增添了他个人的魅力。休·格兰特（Hugh Grant）64岁的脸看起来也依然充满魅力地略显褶皱。也许当每个人都被统一打造成完美无瑕的光滑面容后，松弛、凹凸、粗大毛孔和粗糙肌肤反而会成为最令人向往的特征。到那时，我将准备好迎接我的特写镜头。
hypo_5: 我想我们这些“微整形”抵制者将不得不等待，并希望潮流能够逆转。正如司美格鲁肽和闪亮的瓷贴面可能会让纤瘦和好莱坞式的牙齿显得乏味和普通，或许完美的脸庞也会失宠？我提到瓷贴面，是因为在看《BJ单身日记》电影时，我被切瓦特·埃加福特的牙齿迷住了。它们确实非常可爱，是的，但又有点……正常？下面有一颗牙齿从其他牙齿后面稍微探出头来。这只会增加他的魅力。休·格兰特64岁的脸也显得充满魅力的褶皱。也许当每个人都被同质化成一种完美的光滑亮泽时，松弛、肿块、粗大的毛孔和粗糙的皮肤将成为最令人向往的特质。到那时，我将为我的特写镜头做好准备。
hypo_6: 我想，我们这些拒绝微整形的人将不得不等待，并希望潮流会发生转变。随着奥泽比克和光亮牙贴面可能让苗条和好莱坞牙齿看起来乏味和普通，也许完美的面孔会失去流行？我提到牙贴面，是因为当我观看《布里吉特·琼斯》电影时，我被奇维特尔·伊吉福的牙齿迷住了。它们绝对很漂亮，但某种程度上……很自然？一颗下排牙齿从其他牙齿后面稍微露出来。这只增加了他的魅力。休·格兰特64岁的脸也显得魅力十足地皱巴巴的。也许当每个人都被同质化到完美无瑕的光泽时，下垂、凸起、大毛孔和粗糙斑点将成为最令人向往的东西。到那时，我将准备好我的特写镜头。
hypo_7: 我猜想那些热衷于整形的人们可能需要耐心等待，并寄希望于时尚的转变。 随着奥司麦格（Ozempic）和亮白的瓷贴面牙齿可能让纤瘦和好莱坞式的牙齿显得平庸和普通，或许完美的面容也会失去时尚地位？ 我提及瓷贴面牙齿，是因为观看《BJ单身日记》时，我被奇韦特尔·艾吉奥福（Chiwetel Ejiofor）的牙齿所吸引。 它们确实很漂亮，但却显得……正常？ 他的一个下牙略微从其他牙齿后面露出来，这反而增添了他的魅力。 休·格兰特（Hugh Grant）64岁的脸庞也呈现出一种富有魅力的褶皱感。 也许当每个人都被打造成完美无瑕的形象时，下垂、凸起、毛孔粗大和粗糙的皮肤反而会成为人们追求的极致。 到那时，我将准备好面对镜头。

source_doc: Was it really only a month ago that the pole-dancer patron, fridge explorer, Brexit get-doer, model bus maker, sofa-strainer, wall-spaffer, current Daily Mail columnist and former British prime minister Boris Johnson eulogised the inauguration of Donald Trump in the Mail, recounting how, as the "invisible pulse of power surged" from the battered bible into the hand of Trump: "I saw the moment the world's wokerati had worked so hard to prevent."
hypo_0: 仅仅一个月前，脱衣舞俱乐部常客、冰箱探险家、脱欧推动者、模型巴士制造者、沙发过滤器、墙壁粉刷匠、现任《每日邮报》专栏作家、英国前首相鲍里斯·约翰逊在《每日邮报》上盛赞特朗普的就职典礼，描述当“权力的无形脉动”从破旧的《圣经》传递到特朗普手中时：“我目睹了‘觉醒派’竭力阻止的时刻。”
hypo_1: 就在一个月前，那个热衷于脱衣舞、探索冰箱、推动脱欧、制造模型巴士、用沙发过滤意大利面、在墙上涂鸦的专栏作家、前英国首相鲍里斯·约翰逊还在《每日邮报》上撰文盛赞特朗普的就职典礼，他描述道，当“权力的无形脉搏”从那本破旧的圣经传入特朗普手中时：“我目睹了全球进步人士竭力阻止的那个时刻。”
hypo_2: 就在一个月前，跳钢管舞的赞助人、冰箱探险家、脱欧执行者、模型巴士制造商、沙发拉伸者、墙面拍打者、现任《每日邮报》专栏作家、前英国首相鲍里斯·约翰逊在《邮报》上赞扬了唐纳德·特朗普的就职典礼，讲述了当“无形的权力脉搏”从破旧的圣经中涌向特朗普的手时的情景：“我看到了全世界努力想要阻止的那一刻。”
hypo_3: 难道真的只是一个月前，这位脱衣舞俱乐部常客、冰箱探险家、脱欧推动者、模型巴士制造商、沙发测试者、墙面涂鸦者、现任《每日邮报》专栏作家兼前英国首相鲍里斯·约翰逊在《邮报》上赞美唐纳德·特朗普的就职典礼？他回忆道，当“无形的权力脉动”从那本破旧的圣经涌入特朗普手中时，“我看到了全球‘政治正确者’们竭力想要阻止的那一刻。”
hypo_4: 真的是一个月前吗？那个在《每日邮报》上赞扬唐纳德·特朗普就职典礼的博里斯·约翰逊，他既是极棒舞者赞助人、冰箱探险家、脱欧推动者、模型巴士制造者、沙发测试者、墙壁涂鸦者，也是现任《每日邮报》专栏作家和前英国首相。他在文章中回忆说，当那本破旧的圣经中“无形的权力脉动”涌入特朗普的手中时：“我看到了世界上的觉醒者们努力阻止的那一刻。”
hypo_5: 还记得吗？仅仅一个月前，那位既是钢管舞爱好者、又是冰箱“探险家”、还是“脱欧”倡导者、模型公交车制作者、沙发“瘫痪者”、墙壁“涂鸦者”、现任《每日邮报》专栏作家，同时也是英国前首相的鲍里斯·约翰逊，曾在《每日邮报》上盛赞了唐纳德·特朗普的就职典礼，并描述了当时的情景：当“一股无形的力量”从那本破旧的圣经传递到特朗普手中时，他说：“我亲眼目睹了，那些一直竭尽全力想要阻止这一幕发生的人，最终未能如愿。”
hypo_6: 就在一个多月前，这位舞杆表演者的资助人、冰箱探险家、英国脱欧积极分子、公交模型制造者、沙发挤压机发明者、墙壁装饰设计师、现任《每日邮报》专栏作者、前任英国首相鲍里斯·约翰逊在《邮报》上为唐纳德·特朗普的就职典礼撰写了悼文。他回忆道，在他看来，“一股看不见的力量从那本旧圣经中涌入特朗普的手中，当时，我清楚地看到了全世界为了阻止这一幕而付出艰辛努力的瞬间。”
hypo_7: 难道真是一个月前，那位钢管舞女郎的常客、冰箱探险家、脱欧实干家、模型巴士制作者、沙发压迫者、墙壁涂鸦者、现任《每日邮邮报》专栏作家及前英国首相鲍里斯·约翰逊，还在《邮报》上为唐纳德·特朗普的就职典礼大唱赞歌吗？他回忆道，当“权力的无形脉搏”从那本破旧的圣经涌入特朗普之手时：“我看到了全世界‘觉醒派’曾如此努力阻止的那一刻。”

source_doc: I hope Johnson is pleased with the way things have worked out. Because now the foolish wokerati have been schooled beyond Johnson's wettest dreams. It's the Trump-Putin-bin Salman party! An adjudicated sex offender and convicted fraudster, and a man who sanctioned a chemical warfare hit, killing a British citizen on British soil, have met at the luxury Saudia Arabian hotel of another man, who, according to the US, reportedly approved the murder and subsequent dismemberment of a journalist, to discuss the similarly brutal dismemberment of Ukraine, without consulting either Ukraine itself or the countries most directly affected by the legitimisation of Putin's territorial anxieties. Don't worry, Poland! Stable genius Trump has got this covered, so break out the bone saws, pop the cork on the novichok and grab the girls by the pussy! There are 1970s Italian slasher films with less gruesome plotlines. Well said, Boris Johnson! That's certainly stuck it to the wokerati!
hypo_0: 但愿约翰逊对事态发展感到满意。因为如今"觉醒派"遭受的教训远超他最狂野的想象。这是特朗普-普京-萨勒曼的狂欢派对！一位被裁定的性侵犯者、定罪的欺诈犯，与一位批准使用化学武器在英国领土杀害英国公民的凶手，在另一位据美国指控涉嫌批准谋杀并肢解记者的沙特富豪的豪华酒店里会晤，商讨对乌克兰实施同样残酷的肢解——既未咨询乌克兰，也未顾及受普京领土焦虑合法化直接影响最深的国家。别担心波兰！天才特朗普已掌控全局，快拿出骨锯、打开诺维乔克毒剂的瓶盖、像抓小猫那样抓住女孩们吧！1970年代的意大利恐怖片都没这般骇人剧情。说得好啊，鲍里斯·约翰逊！这真是给"觉醒派"一记响亮的耳光！
hypo_1: 希望约翰逊对事态的发展感到满意。因为现在，那些愚蠢的“觉醒派”被教训得比约翰逊最狂野的梦想还要彻底。这是特朗普-普京-本·萨勒曼的派对！一名被判定的性犯罪者和欺诈犯，以及一名批准化学武器袭击、导致一名英国公民在英国领土上死亡的人，在另一名男子的豪华沙特酒店会面。根据美国的说法，这名男子据称批准了记者的谋杀和肢解，他们讨论了对乌克兰的类似残酷肢解，却没有征询乌克兰本身或受普京领土野心合法化影响最直接的国家的意见。别担心，波兰！稳定天才特朗普已经掌控一切，所以拿出骨锯，打开诺维乔克毒剂，抓住女孩的下体吧！这比1970年代意大利恐怖电影的情节还要血腥。说得好，鲍里斯·约翰逊！这确实让“觉醒派”吃了苦头！
hypo_2: 我希望约翰逊对目前的结果感到满意。因为现在，那些自诩为“进步人士”的人们受到了严厉的教训，这或许超出了约翰逊的预期。特朗普、普京和本·萨尔曼在沙特阿拉伯的一家豪华酒店会面。其中，一名男子曾被法庭裁定为性犯罪者，另一名男子被判犯有欺诈罪，还有一名男子被指控批准了化学武器袭击，导致英国公民在英国本土丧生。这次会晤由另一位人士主持，据美国报告，他涉嫌参与了一名记者的谋杀和后续的残忍处理。他们讨论了乌克兰所面临的同样严峻的局势，而未事先与乌克兰及其受普京领土主张影响最直接的国家进行协商。波兰无需担忧，特朗普会妥善处理此事。令人担忧的是，一系列令人不安的事件正在发生。即使是上世纪七十年代的意大利恐怖片，其情节也显得较为温和。约翰逊，说得好！这无疑对某些观点造成了冲击。
hypo_3: 我希望约翰逊对事情的进展感到满意。因为现在那些愚蠢的“觉醒者”已经被教育得远远超出了约翰逊最疯狂的梦想。这是一场特朗普、普京和本·萨尔曼的盛会！一个被判定为性犯罪者和被定罪的欺诈犯，以及一个批准了化学武器袭击，在英国领土上杀害了一名英国公民的男人，在另一个男人的豪华沙特阿拉伯酒店中会面，讨论了对乌克兰的同样残暴的肢解，而没有咨询乌克兰本身或受普京领土焦虑合法化影响最直接的国家。别担心，波兰！稳定的天才特朗普已经掌控了这一切，所以拿出骨锯，打开诺维乔克毒药的瓶盖，抓住女孩们！1970年代的意大利血腥电影的剧情都比这更温和。说得真好，鲍里斯·约翰逊！这确实狠狠地打击了“觉醒者”们！
hypo_4: 我希望约翰逊对事情的发展感到满意。因为现在愚蠢的觉醒派已经被教训得超出了约翰逊最狂野的梦想。这是特朗普-普京-本·萨勒曼的聚会！一个被裁定的性犯罪者和被定罪的欺诈犯，以及一个批准化学武器袭击、在英国土地上杀死英国公民的人，在另一个人的豪华沙特阿拉伯酒店会面，据美国称，这个人据报批准了对一名记者的谋杀和随后的肢解，讨论对乌克兰的类似残酷肢解，而没有咨询乌克兰本身或受普京领土焦虑合法化最直接影响的国家。别担心，波兰！稳定的天才特朗普已经搞定了，所以拿出骨锯，打开诺维乔克的瓶塞，抓住女孩们的私处！1970年代的意大利恐怖片都没有如此可怕的情节。说得好，鲍里斯·约翰逊！这确实给了觉醒派一记重击！
hypo_5: 希望约翰逊对事态的发展感到满意。因为现在，那些愚蠢的“觉醒人士”已经被教育得比约翰逊最狂热的梦想还要彻底。这是特朗普-普京-本·萨勒曼的聚会！一位被判定为性犯罪者和诈骗犯的人，与一位曾批准化学武器袭击、导致一名英国公民在英国本土丧生的人，在另一位据美国称曾批准谋杀并肢解一名记者的人的豪华沙特阿拉伯酒店会面，讨论同样残酷地肢解乌克兰的问题，而没有征求乌克兰本身或那些最直接受普京领土焦虑合法化影响的国家的意见。别担心，波兰！“稳定的天才”特朗普已经掌控全局，拿出骨锯，打开诺维乔克，抓住女孩！有些上世纪七十年代的意大利血腥片情节都没这么骇人。说得好，鲍里斯·约翰逊！这可真是狠狠地教训了“觉醒人士”！
hypo_6: 但愿约翰逊对目前的事态发展感到满意。如今，“愚蠢的进步派”们所受到的教训，已经远远超出了约翰逊最疯狂的想象。这是一场属于特朗普、普京和本·萨勒曼的“派对”：一位被判定为性犯罪者和诈骗犯的人，一位曾批准使用化学武器、导致一名英国公民在英国领土上死亡的人，与另一位据美国指控涉嫌批准谋杀并肢解一名记者的人，在沙特的一家豪华酒店会面，共同商讨以同样残暴的方式“肢解”乌克兰，而整个过程既未征询乌克兰的意见，也未与那些最直接受到普京“领土焦虑合法化”政策影响的国家进行协商。别担心，波兰！“天才”特朗普已经搞定了一切！就连上世纪70年代的意大利恐怖片的情节也没有这么骇人听闻。说得好，鲍里斯·约翰逊！这可真是狠狠地给了“进步派”一记耳光！
hypo_7: 我希望约翰逊对事态的进展感到满意。因为现在这些愚蠢的政治正确者受到了超乎约翰逊梦想的严厉教训。这里是一场特朗普-普京-本·萨勒曼的聚会！一个被判犯有性罪行和诈骗罪的人，以及一个批准化学武器袭击、在英国领土上杀死一名英国公民的人，来到另一位沙特阿拉伯绅士的豪华酒店会面。根据美国报道，这位绅士还批准了对一名记者的谋杀及随后的肢解。他们在这里讨论同样残酷的乌克兰肢解问题，却没有咨询乌克兰自身的意见，也没有征求受到普京领土焦虑合法化影响最深的国家的意见。别担心，波兰！冷静又天才的特朗普已经搞定这一切了，快拿出骨锯、打开诺维乔克的瓶塞，抓住那些女人的屁股！一些1970年代的意大利虐杀电影的剧情都没有这么可怕。说得好，鲍里斯·约翰逊！这确实让政治正确者们倒了大霉！

source_doc: If only Johnson, and Trump's other cheerleaders in the rightwing press and on the right of the house, could be brave enough to call out Trump for what he is. If only Johnson had the moral courage of Ed Davey from the Liberal Democrats. In what newly warped reality does that sentence even exist? But, on balance, the whitewashing of the deaths of tens of thousands of Ukrainians is a small price to pay for the delight Trump has bought to the smiling faces of people who hate the transgender community, wild swimming enthusiasts and Guardian readers. Sniffing mineral rights in the air, like the smell of napalm in the morning, Trump has grabbed Ukraine by the pussy and he ain't gonna let go. Trump is, unequivocally, the worst thing to happen to human civilisation since Hitler. And Ricky Gervais's After Life.
hypo_0: 要是约翰逊和右翼媒体及议会右翼的其他特朗普支持者能有勇气直言特朗普的本来面目就好了。要是约翰逊能有自由民主党人埃德·戴维那样的道德勇气就好了。在如今这个扭曲的现实中，这句话居然成立？但总的来说，为了让那些讨厌跨性别群体、野外游泳爱好者和《卫报》读者的人们脸上露出笑容，数万乌克兰人死亡被粉饰成小小的代价。特朗普像早晨的汽油味一样嗅到了矿产资源的气息，他已经牢牢抓住乌克兰，绝不会松手。特朗普无疑是自希特勒以来人类文明遭遇的最糟糕的事情。还有瑞奇·热维斯的《来世》。
hypo_1: 要是约翰逊和特朗普在右翼媒体及议会右翼的其他拥趸能鼓起勇气揭露特朗普的真面目就好了。要是约翰逊能有自民党领袖埃德·戴维那样的道德勇气就好了——在何种扭曲的现实里这种假设才能成立？但平心而论，用数万乌克兰人的死亡换取特朗普给反跨性别群体、野外游泳爱好者和《卫报》读者带来的欢愉，这代价还算小的。特朗普嗅着空气中的矿产权利，如同嗅着清晨的凝固汽油弹气息，他像抓小猫般掐住乌克兰的命脉绝不松手。毋庸置疑，特朗普是人类文明自希特勒以来遭遇的最大灾难——瑞奇·热维斯的《后半生》除外。
hypo_2: 如果约翰逊和特朗普在右翼媒体和议会右翼的其他啦啦队员能够勇敢地指出特朗普的真面目就好了。如果约翰逊能有自由民主党埃德·戴维的道德勇气就好了。在什么新扭曲的现实中这句话甚至存在？但总的来说，对数万乌克兰人死亡的粉饰是一个小代价，换取特朗普给那些憎恨跨性别群体、野外游泳爱好者和《卫报》读者的人们脸上带来的喜悦。嗅着空气中的矿物权利，就像早晨凝固汽油弹的味道，特朗普抓住了乌克兰的私处，他不会放手。特朗普毫无疑问是自希特勒以来人类文明发生的最糟糕的事情。还有里基·热尔韦的《来世》。
hypo_3: 如果约翰逊和特朗普在右翼媒体及国会右翼中的其他支持者有足够的勇气揭穿特朗普的真面目就好了。如果约翰逊能具备前自由民主党议员艾德·戴维的道德勇气就好了。在何种新扭曲的现实中，这样的句子竟会存在？但是，总的来看，掩盖数万名乌克兰人死亡的事实只是微不足道的代价，以换取特朗普为那些憎恨跨性别者、热衷野外游泳的人以及《卫报》读者们所带来的欢笑。特朗普嗅到了矿权的味道，就像早晨的纳帕姆燃烧弹的气息一般，他抓住了乌克兰这条命脉，绝不会放手。特朗普无疑是对人类文明自希特勒以来最糟糕的威胁。还有，还有瑞克·格维斯的《天堂来信》。
hypo_4: 要是约翰逊，以及右翼媒体和议会右派中特朗普的其他支持者们，能有勇气揭露特朗普的真面目就好了。要是约翰逊能有自由民主党人埃德·戴维那样的道德勇气就好了。在怎样一个新扭曲的现实中，这句话才可能存在？但总的来说，为了给那些憎恨跨性别群体、热衷野外游泳的人以及《卫报》读者的人们带来特朗普式的愉悦，粉饰数万乌克兰人的死亡只是个小小的代价。特朗普在空气中嗅到了矿产权利，就像早晨闻到凝固汽油弹的气味一样，他抓住了乌克兰的私处，而且不打算放手。毫无疑问，特朗普是自希特勒以来人类文明遭遇的最糟糕的事情。还有瑞奇·热维斯的《后半生》。
hypo_5: 如果约翰逊和特朗普在右翼媒体和众议院右翼的其他支持者能够勇敢地指出特朗普的真实面目就好了。如果约翰逊具有自由民主党人埃德·戴维的道德勇气就好了。在这个新扭曲的现实中，这样的句子竟然存在？但是，总的来说，数万乌克兰人的死亡被粉饰成了一个小代价，为的是让特朗普给那些仇恨跨性别人群、狂野游泳爱好者和《卫报》读者的笑容带来欢乐。特朗普像闻到早晨的凝固汽油弹气味一样，嗅到了空气中的矿产权气味，他抓住了乌克兰的隐私部位，不肯放手。特朗普无疑是自希特勒以来人类文明面临的最糟糕的事情。还有瑞奇·热维斯的《死后人生》。
hypo_6: 如果约翰逊和特朗普在右翼媒体和国会右翼的其他吹捧者都能勇敢地揭露特朗普的真面目该有多好。如果约翰逊能像自由民主党的埃德·戴维一样有道德勇气。在这个扭曲的现实中，这句话甚至存在吗？不过，从总体上来看，掩盖数万名乌克兰人死亡的事实，是为了换取特朗普给那些讨厌变性人、野泳爱好者和《卫报》读者带来的喜悦而付出的小小代价。特朗普嗅到了矿产权的气息，就像早晨闻到罂粟花的香味一样，他抓住了乌克兰，而且不会放手。毫无疑问，特朗普是自希特勒以来对人类文明造成的最严重的威胁。还有瑞奇·杰维斯的《后世》。
hypo_7: 要是约翰逊，以及特朗普在右翼媒体和议会右翼的其他啦啦队长们，能有足够的勇气，直斥特朗普的真面目就好了。要是约翰逊能有自由民主党埃德·戴维的道德勇气就好了。在如今这个扭曲的现实中，这句话怎么可能存在？但是，总的来说，为了那些憎恨跨性别群体、野泳爱好者和《卫报》读者的人脸上露出的笑容，粉饰数万乌克兰人的死亡只是一个小小的代价。特朗普像闻到清晨凝固汽油弹的气味一样，在空气中嗅探着矿产权利。他抓住了乌克兰的私处，而且绝不会放手。特朗普，毫无疑问，是继希特勒之后人类文明发生的最糟糕的事情。还有瑞奇·热维斯的《后半生》。

source_doc: European politicians more rational and less self-serving than Johnson are trying to formulate the correct response to Trump's rapid and reckless redrawing of the postwar world disorder in his own, and Russia's, interests. The correct response is to shit your pants. On Tuesday, Trump even blamed Ukraine itself for being invaded, which is a bit like blaming E Jean Carroll herself for being sexually abused in a department store changing room. Couldn't she have cut a pre-emptive deal before things escalated? Victims! Always blaming someone else. But Trump has put the idea that the invasion of Ukraine is Ukraine's fault out there now, on the world stage, amplified by his collaborators in the tech bro media, and it will gradually calcify into one of those persistent alternative facts. By Wednesday he'd called Zelenskyy a dictator (and a mediocre comedian, which in my opinion is even worse).
hypo_0: 与约翰逊相比，一些欧洲政治家更加理性、也更少以个人利益为出发点，他们正在努力寻找应对特朗普以自身和俄罗斯的利益为目的，迅速且鲁莽地重塑战后世界秩序的正确方式。而正确的应对方式，就是感到恐惧。周二，特朗普甚至指责乌克兰“自作自受”，这就像指责E·让·卡罗尔（E Jean Carroll）在百货商店的更衣室里遭到性侵一样荒谬。难道她不应该事先采取预防措施，以避免事态升级吗？受害者！总是把责任推给别人。但特朗普已经将“乌克兰入侵是乌克兰的错”这一观点抛了出来，并且在世界舞台上大肆宣扬，他的观点还得到了科技媒体的推波助澜，最终这种观点很可能会成为一种根深蒂固的“另类事实”。到了周三，他又称泽连斯基为“独裁者”（而且还是个“平庸的喜剧演员”，我认为这比“独裁者”更糟糕）。
hypo_1: 比约翰逊更理性、更不自私的欧洲政客们正试图制定对特朗普迅速而鲁莽地重塑战后世界秩序以符合他和俄罗斯利益的正确回应。正确的回应是吓得裤子都湿了。周二，特朗普甚至指责乌克兰自己导致被入侵，这就像指责E·让·卡罗尔在百货商店试衣间里被性侵。她难道不能在事态升级前达成预防性协议吗？受害者！总是怪别人。但特朗普已经在世界舞台上散布了乌克兰入侵是乌克兰自己的错的观点，并通过科技兄弟媒体的合作者放大，这一观点将逐渐凝固成持久的另类事实。到周三，他称泽连斯基是独裁者（还说他是个平庸的喜剧演员，在我看来这更糟）。
hypo_2: 比约翰逊更理性、更不自私的欧洲政治家们，正试图对特朗普为了自己和俄罗斯的利益，迅速而鲁莽地重绘战后世界乱局的行为，作出正确的回应。正确的回应是吓尿裤子。周二，特朗普甚至指责乌克兰本身就该为被入侵负责，这有点像指责E·让·卡罗尔本人就该为在百货公司更衣室被性侵负责一样。难道她不能在事态升级前达成一个先发制人的协议吗？受害者！总是怪罪别人。但特朗普现在已经把乌克兰被入侵是乌克兰自己的错这个想法，抛向了世界舞台，并且通过他在科技兄弟媒体中的合作者加以放大，这个想法将逐渐僵化，成为那些顽固的“另类事实”之一。到周三，他已经称泽连斯基为独裁者（以及一个平庸的喜剧演员，在我看来这更糟糕）。
hypo_3: 比约翰逊更理性、更少私心的欧洲政客正试图对特朗普为自身及俄罗斯利益而肆意重构战后世界秩序的行为作出恰当回应。最恰当的反应就是吓得屁滚尿流。周二特朗普甚至指责乌克兰招致入侵，这就像指责E·琼·卡罗尔在百货店试衣间遭遇性侵是自找的——她难道不能在事态恶化前达成预防性协议吗？这些受害者啊，总爱怪罪别人。但特朗普已将"乌克兰该为入侵负责"的论调搬上世界舞台，经科技兄弟媒体的同谋放大，终将固化为那些顽固的另类事实之一。到了周三，他又称泽连斯基是独裁者（还说是平庸的喜剧演员，要我说这更恶劣）。
hypo_4: 与约翰逊相比，更为理性、不那么自私的欧洲政客们正试图制定正确的回应，以应对特朗普为了他自己和俄罗斯的利益，迅速且鲁莽地重新绘制战后世界秩序。正确的回应是束紧裤带。周二，特朗普甚至指责乌克兰本身被入侵，这有点像指责E·简·卡罗尔自己在百货商店更衣室遭到性侵犯。她难道不能在事情升级之前就达成先发制人的协议吗？受害者！总是把责任推给别人。但特朗普现在已经在世界舞台上提出了乌克兰入侵是乌克兰的错的观点，他的科技媒体合作者们对此进行了放大，这一观点将逐渐演变成其中一个持续的另类事实。到周三，他已将泽连斯基称为独裁者（以及一名平庸的喜剧演员，在我看来，这更糟糕）。
hypo_5: 比约翰逊更理性、更少自私的欧洲政治家们正在试图制定正确的应对措施，以应对特朗普迅速而鲁莽地按照他自己和俄罗斯的利益重新划定战后世界秩序的行为。正确的应对方式是吓尿裤子。周二，特朗普甚至将乌克兰被入侵的责任归咎于乌克兰本身，这有点像将伊·让·卡罗尔在百货公司更衣室遭受性侵犯的责任归咎于她自己。难道她不能在事态升级之前达成预防性协议吗？受害者！总是责怪别人。但特朗普现在已经在国际舞台上将入侵乌克兰的责任归咎于乌克兰，他的科技兄弟媒体中的合作者们放大了这一观点，它将逐渐凝固为那些持续存在的替代事实之一。到周三，他已经称泽连斯基为独裁者（以及一个平庸的喜剧演员，在我看来，这甚至更糟）。
hypo_6: 欧洲政界人士，相比约翰逊，显得更为理性且不以自我为中心，正试图就特朗普迅速且鲁莽地重塑二战后世界秩序这一行为作出恰当回应，而这一行为明显符合特朗普及其俄罗斯的利益。适当的回应方式是令人感到无力和震惊。周二，特朗普甚至指责乌克兰自身遭到入侵，这就像指责伊 Jean Carroll 在百货商店更衣室遭受性侵犯一样。难道她不能在事态升级前采取预防措施吗？受害者！总是将责任推卸给他人。但特朗普现在已经将入侵乌克兰是乌克兰自身之过这一观点抛向了世界舞台，并被其在科技媒体中的支持者所放大，它将逐渐演变成一种难以改变的错误认知。到周三，他已经将泽连斯基称为独裁者（同时，他认为泽连斯基是一位普通的喜剧演员）。
hypo_7: 欧洲政客比约翰逊更理性、更不自私，试图对特朗普在自身和俄罗斯的利益下，急速、鲁莽地重塑战后世界秩序的举动做出恰当反应。恰当的反应是束手无策。周二，特朗普甚至指责乌克兰自己发动入侵，这就跟他指责贾恩·卡罗尔在百货公司更衣室里遭受性侵犯一样荒唐。她难道不能在事情升级之前就与对方达成预先协议吗？受害者们总是将责任推脱给别人！但特朗普现在已经在世界舞台上将“乌克兰入侵是乌克兰自己的责任”这个观点放了出来，由他的盟友在科技博主媒体上大力推波助澜，该观点将逐步成为一种顽固的“另类事实”。周三，他将泽连斯基称为独裁者（以及一个平庸的喜剧演员，我认为这样称呼更甚于前者）。

source_doc: Think what Hitler could have achieved if he'd had Twitter, currently X, and Google at his disposal
hypo_0: 想象一下，如果希特勒当年能够使用推特（现称 X）和谷歌这样的工具，他能干出什么事来呢？
